_meta.echiquier_attribue_a: al-Shaykh al-Akbar (Ibn ʿArabī), qaddasa Llāhu sirrahu — attribution traditionnelle, à prendre avec prudence
_meta.commentaire_titre: Uns al-khāʾifīn wa samīr al-ʿākifīn fī sharḥ shaṭranj al-ʿārifīn
_meta.commentateur: Muḥammad b. al-Hāshimī b. ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Ḥasanī al-Tilimsānī puis al-Dimashqī
_meta.avertissement: Reconstruction de travail issue du plateau scanné et du commentaire publié par Jean-Louis Michon (ARChè, 1998). Les 100 cases sont vérifiées dans le texte arabe ; les mouvements ne sont affichés que lorsqu'ils sont confirmés.
_meta.validation_en_cours: État actuel : les 100 cases sont vérifiées dans le texte arabe de Michon/al-Hāshimī, et 15 mouvements (montées, chaînes et grappins) sont confirmés. Le travail restant concerne surtout l'enrichissement pédagogique des fiches et le tracé progressif des mouvements.

cases.resume: The radical starting point: the servant does not possess his being, he receives it.
cases.explication_simple: Possible non-being, the counterpart of possible existence: it subsists in the divine Knowledge while being inexistent in itself, and can move towards existence.
cases.explication_spirituelle: This square lays down the metaphysical foundation of the path: everything that is not God is possible, dependent, called from inexistence towards existence. It teaches the traveller not to begin with the pride of being someone, but with the lucidity of being received at every instant.
cases.mecanisme: The journey begins when the human being recognises that his existence is not a personal property but a continual gift of divine Power.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that recognising one's non-being means despising oneself, when in fact it is about understanding one's ontological dependence.
remede: Return to gratitude for the existence received.
Distinguish humility from self-deprecation.
Ask God to make the being received into a living obedience.
cases.question_introspection: What do I attribute to myself as though I were its source?
cases.pratique: Pause for a few moments on this inner sentence: my being is entrusted to me. Observe what, within oneself, claims existence as a possession.
symptomes: A feeling of wanting to possess everything, even one's own being.
Forgetting one's fundamental dependence upon God.
Difficulty in receiving life as a deposit rather than as a due.
cases.ouverture: Possible non-being opens onto the first humility: I am not the source of myself.

cases.resume: The passage from the possible to existence: being appears through Power and Wisdom.
cases.explication_simple: Entry into existence: through pure Power (Adam, the Spirits, the Angels), or through Wisdom by way of progenitors.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The birth of existence recalls that God brings beings into existence either through His direct Power or through the mediations of His Wisdom. Here the traveller learns to respect causes without worshipping them: they serve manifestation, but are not the ultimate source.
cases.mecanisme: After possible non-being, the being is brought into existence by a decree that precedes its will and by mediations that inscribe it within the world.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that natural mediations suffice to explain the mystery of appearance.
remede: Honour causes without attributing independence to them.
Give thanks for the life received.
Orient existence towards that for which it was given.
cases.question_introspection: If my existence is given, to what am I called to devote it?
cases.pratique: Reread one's own history as a succession of gifts and mediations. Name a visible cause, then recognise the Source upon which it depends.
symptomes: Astonishment before the fact of existing.
Confusion between visible causes and the invisible Source.
A tendency to reduce birth to a mere material mechanism.
cases.ouverture: To see birth as a sign transforms existence into responsibility.

cases.resume: The first ease: existence becomes pleasant and the heart can settle into it.
cases.explication_simple: The door of contentment (or “this lower world”): the natural inclination to be satisfied with the world and to love it — which pushes towards passion.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Contentment is ambivalent: it may be gratitude, but also attachment to the world. In the commentary, ease with regard to existence pushes towards passion if it is not educated. The beginner must understand that all sweetness must be referred back to God so that it does not become a snare.
cases.mecanisme: The servant discovers the ease of existing and risks confusing the rest that is given with settling into the world.
cases.illusion_possible: To take the peace of contentment for a permission to follow everything that pleases.
remede: Give thanks at the very moment the sweetness appears.
Check whether the ease increases obedience or negligence.
Keep an inner sobriety in pleasant moments.
cases.question_introspection: Which received sweetness becomes in me a discreet attachment?
cases.pratique: Before a satisfaction, ask: does this joy draw me closer to God or does it shut me into the object?
symptomes: A taste for life that becomes a search for comfort.
Difficulty in leaving an immediate satisfaction.
A tendency to forget the Giver within the gift.
cases.ouverture: Contentment becomes right when it is transformed into gratitude and not into inner sleep.

cases.resume: The animal thrust of desire when it ceases to be ordered.
cases.explication_simple: Animal passion, which draws the servant towards the next stage.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Passion is not merely the existence of desire; it is desire that governs. It lowers the servant because it makes him seek relief before truth. The Chessboard places here a decisive threshold: vital energy must be educated, otherwise it becomes an engine of the fall.
cases.mecanisme: Ease in existence is distorted when the heart seeks first the satisfaction of the senses or of the imagination.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that being sincere with oneself means obeying all one's impulses.
remede: Name the desire without obeying it immediately.
Seek sobriety, modesty and measure.
Return to a simple act of bodily obedience.
cases.question_introspection: Which desire becomes master the moment I cease to look it in the face?
cases.pratique: When a passion rises, defer the response, breathe, then ask what deeper hunger hides beneath the impulse.
symptomes: An impulse that wants to be followed without delay.
Inner justifications to make licit what troubles.
Loss of perspective before a strong craving.
cases.ouverture: Passion reveals an energy that can be purified and reoriented towards the desire for God.

cases.resume: The lowering produced by the disordered search for satisfaction.
cases.explication_simple: He debases himself by dint of satisfying his passion, and his degradation deepens down to the lowest depths.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Abasement appears when desire makes one lose the dignity of the servant. One falls not only through the visible act, but through the inner acceptance of selling oneself to whatever promises enjoyment or power. This square teaches that spiritual dignity depends on orientation, not on social image.
cases.mecanisme: Passion, when followed, forces the servant to negotiate with what abases him.
cases.illusion_possible: To think that humiliation will disappear once the desire is finally satisfied.
remede: Break with the object that buys dignity.
Recall that the servant's honour lies in his dependence upon God.
Make a concrete act of taking oneself back in hand.
cases.question_introspection: To what do I give the power to diminish my inner dignity?
cases.pratique: Identify a small concession that abases the heart and decide on a clear limit for the day.
symptomes: Repeated compromises with what one knows to be inferior.
A diffuse shame after yielding to a craving.
Dependence on a gaze, a habit or a pleasure.
cases.ouverture: To see abasement can become the beginning of a true return to dignity before God.

cases.resume: The lowest depths: the soul feels itself drawn beneath the earth by abasement.
cases.explication_simple: The lowest depths to which abasement has led him; if nothing helps him, he is led to blind impulsiveness.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Beneath the Earth is not a definitive condemnation but the image of a burial. When the servant descends too low, he no longer sees the horizon and believes that the present state is all of reality. The pedagogy of the Chessboard nevertheless remains open: even the lowest depths can awaken the call for help.
cases.mecanisme: Prolonged abasement produces an impression of confinement in which the soul no longer knows how to climb back up.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that the depth of the fall annuls the possibility of mercy.
remede: Do not despair of Mercy.
Ask for help before hardening.
Take up again a very simple act of purification or obedience.
cases.question_introspection: Which part of me believes itself too low to be reached by God?
cases.pratique: Write down what seems impossible to raise up, then formulate a brief request for help, without heroic discourse.
symptomes: A feeling of being stuck in a low condition.
Loss of taste for noble acts.
The belief that recovery is no longer possible.
cases.ouverture: The lowest depths teach that no one rises again through pride; one rises through help asked for and received.

cases.resume: Ignorant aggressiveness: the soul reacts without light or restraint.
cases.explication_simple: He displays his impulsiveness and his aggressiveness towards people, who return it to him: “each receives the like of his own actions”.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Blind impulsiveness is ignorance in act. It does not know how to read consequences, it attacks, then turns the reaction of others into proof against them. The commentary shows a chain: aggressiveness, reprisals, dissimulation, hatred.
cases.mecanisme: When the buried soul receives no light, it compensates through brutality and immediate reaction.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe oneself frank or strong when one is merely governed by impulse.
remede: Suspend the reaction.
Seek the counsel of a composed person.
Quickly repair the harm caused by a word or a gesture.
cases.question_introspection: In what situation does my quick reaction deprive me of light?
cases.pratique: Before responding to an annoyance, wait long enough to be able to name the emotion without acting it out.
symptomes: Responding before having understood.
Taking all opposition as a humiliation.
Hiding one's aggressiveness while awaiting an occasion for revenge.
cases.ouverture: To recognise impulsiveness allows one to recover reason, adab and responsibility.

cases.resume: The hidden rancour that bides its time.
cases.explication_simple: He hates those who do him harm, in reprisal for his own coarseness.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Hatred is born here of thwarted aggressiveness. The wounded soul keeps the evil instead of handing it over to God; it inwardly prepares evil deeds. This square teaches that what remains buried in the heart often ends by taking shape in acts.
cases.mecanisme: When impulsiveness meets a resistance, it can withdraw into a secret hostility.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that keeping rancour protects, when in fact it shuts the heart up with the offence.
remede: Entrust the judgement to God.
Cease to feed the wounding scene mentally.
Make an invocation of deliverance for oneself and, if possible, for the other.
cases.question_introspection: Which wound do I transform into a right to hate?
cases.pratique: Name a precise rancour and observe how it colours one's thoughts, then ask to be freed from its grip.
symptomes: Brooding over the harm received.
Imagining scenes of revenge.
Inwardly rejoicing at another's weakness.
cases.ouverture: Hatred seen in time can become a request for the purification of the heart.

cases.resume: Inner evil becomes visible behaviour.
cases.explication_simple: Evil deeds made outward — insults, ill-treatment: “whoever hides evil in his heart, God clothes him in it”.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Evil deeds are the outward emergence of an unpurified heart. The commentary gives examples of insults and ill-treatment: the evil kept ends by clothing the person. The square obliges one not to separate the inner from the outer.
cases.mecanisme: Hidden hatred seeks an occasion and becomes word, gesture, injustice or harm.
cases.illusion_possible: To think that the evil act relieves the inner wound.
remede: Recognise the act without disguising it.
Repair as far as possible.
Return to the mastery of the tongue and of gestures.
cases.question_introspection: Which inner evil is beginning to become visible in my way of acting?
cases.pratique: Take stock of a recent harmful word or act and choose a concrete reparation.
symptomes: Words that wound deliberately.
Small cruelties justified by irritation.
The desire to make the other feel what one has undergone.
cases.ouverture: The visibility of the act allows responsibility, and therefore reparation.

cases.resume: The outward trial that manifests the consequence of an inner disorder.
cases.explication_simple: He falls into trial: prison, corporal punishments, exile, poverty, the contempt of his fellows.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The trial is not presented as a mere mechanical punishment. It may be prison, poverty, humiliation or constriction: it reveals the fragility of the soul and can provoke either bitterness or repentance. The way one passes through the trial decides what follows.
cases.mecanisme: Evil deeds expose the servant to consequences that constrain him and unveil him.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that the trial authorises the loss of adab.
remede: Seek the lesson without crushing oneself.
Return to repentance and patience.
Ask for an outcome that increases uprightness.
cases.question_introspection: When I am tried, what is revealed of my real adab?
cases.pratique: In a present difficulty, distinguish what depends on me, what does not depend on me, and what God is teaching me through this limit.
symptomes: Feeling life as closed or punitive.
Believing oneself merely a victim of circumstances.
Reacting to the trial with hardness rather than with return.
cases.ouverture: The trial can become a school if it brings one back to lucidity and not to revolt.

cases.resume: The loss of inner bearing before God, creatures and oneself.
cases.explication_simple: His adab dwindles, as does his modesty towards God and creatures: he comes to find it good to act badly.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Lack of adab is a dangerous square because it trivialises disorder. In the trial, a person may judge good what is not, and no longer feel that he is acting badly towards God, creatures and himself. It is also a grapnel: it brings one back to evil deeds.
cases.mecanisme: Under the pressure of the trial, modesty, humility and restraint diminish; the soul then begins to justify what it would previously have recognised as a lack of bearing.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that difficulty gives the right to step out of adab.
remede: Pass through the trial again without settling into it.
Recall that the believer learns from what has bitten him.
Return to a measured word and a just gesture.
cases.question_introspection: In which trial do I allow myself to lack bearing?
cases.pratique: Choose today a situation where keeping silence, modesty or gentleness will be a form of inner struggle.
symptomes: Speaking without respect on the pretext of suffering.
Allowing oneself attitudes one used to condemn.
Losing delicacy towards God and others.
cases.ouverture: The return to adab restores dignity even when the outer situation remains difficult.
cases.chemin_texte: » Thus, he finds it good to act badly towards God, towards all the creatures of God and towards himself, and he scarcely realises that the grapnel of evil deeds seizes him and drags him from “lack of adab” to “evil deeds”, making him descend two abysses and find himself a second time at the edge of the trial.

cases.resume: The failure to honour the deposit entrusted by God and by the Prophet.
cases.explication_simple: He betrays God, the Prophet and his commitment — “God does not guide the cunning of traitors” (Quran XII, 52).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Betrayal is deeper than a mere lie: it breaks a known trust. The text recalls that man may betray God, the Prophet and the deposits entrusted to him. It marks a rupture between what one knows and what one does.
cases.mecanisme: When adab diminishes, commitments become negotiable and the soul seeks a ruse to avoid keeping its word.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that a discreet betrayal leaves no trace in the heart.
remede: Identify the deposit entrusted.
Keep a small promise without waiting to be seen.
Ask forgiveness and repair the trust when possible.
cases.question_introspection: Which deposit has been entrusted to me that I treat as though it belonged to me?
cases.pratique: Write down a commitment not kept and today make one tiny but real act to return to it.
symptomes: Promises forgotten because they cost.
Deposits neglected: time, word, responsibility, relationship.
Inner cunning to excuse oneself from a clear commitment.
cases.ouverture: Returning to the deposit repairs inner trust and gives the path consistency again.

cases.resume: Blameworthy acts that degrade human nobility.
cases.explication_simple: Actions blamed by the Law and contrary to dignity: open disobediences whose harm falls only upon him.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Detestable deeds designate disobediences that damage dignity, even when they do not directly harm others. The text obliges one to see that man can destroy himself through gestures he trivialises. Spiritual nobility also passes through the concrete bearing of the body.
cases.mecanisme: The betrayal of deposits opens the door to behaviours that the Law and human dignity reprove.
cases.illusion_possible: To think that an act with no apparent victim has no effect on the soul.
remede: Cut off the occasions that facilitate the act.
Return to bodily and behavioural modesty.
Seek reliable accompaniment if the habit is rooted.
cases.question_introspection: Which trivialised act silently diminishes my inner nobility?
cases.pratique: Spot a habit that weakens dignity and concretely arrange one's environment so as not to feed it.
symptomes: Habits one hides yet protects.
The trivialisation of acts that diminish modesty.
A separation between displayed spirituality and real conduct.
cases.ouverture: To see the blameworthy act as a degradation allows one to choose nobility rather than complacency.

cases.resume: The fire of anger and of its acts.
cases.explication_simple: The fire of anger: “anger is a brand from the fire of hell”. The prohibition bears only on the acts committed under its grip.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Hell is read here through anger: an inner fire, an inflammation of the body, a door opened to acts that draw down wrath. The pedagogy is very concrete: fire is not fought with another fire, but through refuge, water, mastery and prophetic counsel.
cases.mecanisme: Blameworthy acts feed an inner heat that manifests as anger and violence.
cases.illusion_possible: To confuse anger for God's sake with the irritation of the ego.
remede: Seek refuge in God against Satan.
Fall silent, change one's position, perform ablution if one follows this ritual framework.
Defer any decision taken in the flare-up.
cases.question_introspection: Which fire do I call justice when it is consuming me?
cases.pratique: Observe the first bodily signs of anger and prepare a response of extinction before the next flare-up.
symptomes: A body that flares up in anger.
A harsh word that wants to burn the other.
A religious or moral justification of a personal rage.
cases.ouverture: Anger seen as fire allows one to seek extinction rather than explosion.

cases.resume: Entry into what is forbidden or destructive.
cases.explication_simple: He commits every forbidden thing, thinking that God will not accept his repentance — despair of mercy.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Forbidden things represent the loss of boundary. After the fire of anger, the soul frequents the places of perdition and consumes what damages it. This square recalls that the path needs real limits, not only good intentions.
cases.mecanisme: When anger and blameworthy acts dominate, the protective thresholds become weak.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that one can play with the forbidden without being altered by it.
remede: Move away the immediate causes of the fall.
Restore a clear and simple boundary.
Replace the forbidden habit with a licit and living act.
cases.question_introspection: Which protective limit have I turned into an option?
cases.pratique: Name a door to close concretely: place, screen, relationship, time slot or object that facilitates the fall.
symptomes: Frequenting what reawakens the fall.
Deliberately approaching risky situations.
Turning an exception into a habit.
cases.ouverture: The limit is not a prison: it protects the possibility of return.

cases.resume: The sea of perplexity where the soul risks drowning.
cases.explication_simple: Anger and anxiety come together: he drowns as in a great ocean, in quest of a deliverance. (Variant: anger.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: The great ocean can be the image of an immensity that exceeds one. There one can be purified or engulfed. In the Chessboard, settling into it becomes a grapnel: confusion, the forbidden and the ruins bring one back towards a low repetition.
cases.mecanisme: Frequenting the forbidden and the loss of limits plunge the soul into a confused immensity.
cases.illusion_possible: To confuse confusion with spiritual depth.
remede: Return to a shore: a simple act, a clear rule, a sound company.
Do not take great decisions in a state of inner drowning.
Ask God for firm ground.
cases.question_introspection: In which ocean do I remain because clarity would oblige me to choose?
cases.pratique: When everything becomes blurred, reduce the way to a single just act to accomplish now.
symptomes: No longer knowing which direction to take.
Being tossed about by contradictory emotions.
Seeking a depth that becomes engulfment.
cases.ouverture: Perplexity can become a call for help if it does not become a dwelling.
cases.chemin_texte: if he stops and settles into the consumption of forbidden things, he is seized by the grapnel of the great ocean which makes him descend thirty-one abysses, passing through detestable deeds (No. 13), evil deeds (No. 9), the ruins (No. 45), passion (No. 4) to make him fall back into the great ocean.

cases.resume: The sorrow of lack: sincere if it drives towards return, vain if it replaces action.
cases.explication_simple: He laments what he has missed. Well lived, this regret becomes the longing of the sincere that brings one back; otherwise, it is mendacious.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Regret can be a grace or an illusion. The text distinguishes sincere longing, which seeks the means to return, from mendacious sorrow, which sighs without obeying. This square trains the heart not to take spiritual emotion for transformation.
cases.mecanisme: After confusion or privation, the soul regrets what it has missed or lost.
cases.illusion_possible: To take the pain of lack for an effective return.
remede: Transform a sorrow into a concrete step.
Seek the real means of return.
Refuse complacency in the sigh.
cases.question_introspection: Does my regret set me in motion, or does it merely give me the impression of being deep?
cases.pratique: For each regret, write down the corresponding means: what can I do today that is proportionate and true?
symptomes: Sighing over one's state without changing direction.
Feeling spiritual because one is sad.
Regretting obedience while avoiding its means.
cases.ouverture: Regret becomes true when it is converted into means, resolution and act.

cases.resume: The constricted character that makes one's presence difficult for others.
cases.explication_simple: His breast tightens, his character becomes corrupted; he is irritated by observations and hides to avoid reproaches. (Variant: slander, al-ṭaʿn.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Bad character is born here of an unconverted sorrow. The breast tightens, irritation arises, the person hides instead of receiving the reproach. The square shows that spiritual work is verified in the quality of relationship.
cases.mecanisme: When regret remains mendacious, it embitters the soul and corrupts one's way of being.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that one suffers too much to still owe gentleness.
remede: Receive a remark without defending oneself immediately.
Ask forgiveness for relational hardness.
Practise a concrete gentleness towards a close person.
cases.question_introspection: Who most directly endures my inner constriction?
cases.pratique: Choose a relationship where one tenses up and introduce into it a simple gesture of good character.
symptomes: Irritability before remarks.
Withdrawing to avoid being corrected.
Holding others responsible for one's own constriction.
cases.ouverture: Bad character unveiled can become a school of patience, modesty and reparation.

cases.resume: The gap between the outward shown and the inner reality.
cases.explication_simple: To show oneself outwardly other than one is inwardly — in the broad sense, more general than the hypocrisy sanctioned by the Law.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Hypocrisy is taken here in the broad sense: to manifest oneself other than one is. The danger is twofold: to hide one's trouble and to be mistaken about the nature of that trouble. The soul then loses the unity between inner truth and appearance.
cases.mecanisme: Bad character, when it wants to avoid reproaches and preserve its image, fabricates a façade where the outward no longer corresponds to the real inner.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that saving the appearance saves the soul.
remede: Speak truly to God about one's state.
Reduce the gap between word and conduct.
Seek a sincere discretion rather than a perfect image.
cases.question_introspection: Where does my spiritual image protect an unworked zone?
cases.pratique: Spot a phrase or a posture that artificially embellishes my state, then return to a simpler word.
symptomes: Displaying a peace one does not really work at.
Speaking of virtues one refuses in relationships.
Hiding behind a spiritual image.
cases.ouverture: Hypocrisy seen without despair opens onto humble sincerity.

cases.resume: The scrupulous whispering that contracts worship.
cases.explication_simple: The obsessive whispering, born of an excess of scruple in worship: a feeling of imperfection that contracts the soul (qabḍ). (Variant: piety, taqwā.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: The waswās is born of a piety become excessive and anxious. The soul believes it never does enough, then contracts to the point of risking confusion. The text offers a way out: flight towards God, sound company, measured expansion and remembrance.
cases.mecanisme: The inner gap feeds scruples that invade worship and the relation to God.
cases.illusion_possible: To take religious anxiety for depth or vigilance.
remede: Seek refuge in God and cut off the dialogue with the obsession.
Occupy oneself with a clear act rather than an endless checking.
Recover a balanced and soothing company.
cases.question_introspection: Which anxiety do I confuse with presence to God?
cases.pratique: When a spiritual obsession returns, do not negotiate with it: return to a brief invocation and to the present act.
symptomes: Beginning again endlessly for fear of having done badly.
Turning piety into permanent anxiety.
Losing the simplicity of the heart before God.
cases.ouverture: The whispering reveals the need for a simpler trust and a heart less centred on its performance.

cases.resume: The opening of the heart after contraction, with its own adab.
cases.explication_simple: Joy, rejoicing, opening of the heart (inshirāḥ), the opposite of contraction (qabḍ). It has its adab: without it, the steps slip. (Variant: drunkenness, sukr.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Expansion is a joy that is given, but the text warns: it has an etiquette. Without humility, silence and gratitude, joy becomes slipping. It must give the soul rest without settling it into dispersion.
cases.mecanisme: After the contraction of waswās, God may open the heart through a joy, a breathing and a breadth.
cases.illusion_possible: To become attached to the sensation of grace and to believe it acquired.
remede: Keep silence about the state received.
Intensify dhikr and gratitude.
Avoid the company that turns opening into distraction.
cases.question_introspection: When my heart opens, do I become more humble or more scattered?
cases.pratique: In a spiritual joy, choose calm: give thanks, fall silent a little, and accomplish a discreet act.
symptomes: A joyful surge after a heavy period.
The wish to speak too quickly of one's state.
The risk of confusing opening with permission to scatter oneself.
cases.ouverture: Expansion teaches one to receive joy with modesty.

cases.resume: The desire to receive from creatures what should be asked of God.
cases.explication_simple: To desire a thing without employing the just means. “You are free of what you despair of, a slave to what you covet” (Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Covetousness is defined as wanting without employing the just means. It makes one a slave to what one hopes for from people. The text links it to doubt in Providence and to the acquisition of baseness.
cases.mecanisme: Ill-guarded expansion turns towards creatures and begins to expect from them recognition, help or possession.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that possessing the coveted object will give freedom.
remede: Redirect the request towards God.
Employ licit means without inwardly begging.
Practise dignity in lack.
cases.question_introspection: Of what am I a slave because I covet it in creatures?
cases.pratique: Identify an excessive expectation of someone and transform it into a request addressed to God, then into a concrete means.
symptomes: Depending on a response, a gaze or an advantage.
Feeling diminished when people do not give.
Seeking the gift without accepting the just means.
cases.ouverture: To despair of what does not belong to creatures frees the heart.

cases.resume: Figurative love: a sensible attraction that can become a passage towards the real.
cases.explication_simple: Enamoured of the beauty and the beneficence of creatures. If it takes hold of the heart, it can raise it and transport it to real desire (square 44).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Figurative desire is not merely condemned: in the Chessboard, if it truly seizes the heart, it can be raised towards real desire. It shows that created beauty can awaken a higher thirst, provided one does not stop at the form.
cases.mecanisme: Covetousness of creatures transforms into amorous attachment to their beauty or their beneficence.
cases.illusion_possible: To take the symbol for the goal and to shut love up in the created object.
remede: Recognise beauty as a sign.
Do not possess what is meant to orient.
Ask that the attachment be purified into true love.
cases.question_introspection: Which created beauty calls me towards God, and where do I hold it back for myself?
cases.pratique: Before a beauty that attracts, say inwardly: this points to something greater than itself. Observe whether love widens or narrows.
symptomes: Idealising a person or a form.
Reading the inner lack through an attraction.
Confusing the beauty received with the Source of beauty.
cases.ouverture: Figurative love can become a ladder if it reveals the desire for the Real.
cases.chemin_texte: Thus, he traverses in a single step twenty-one stations and, in his progression, he passes through a part of the desired object and through impotence (al-ʿajz), then he stops at real desire.

cases.resume: The perplexity of desire: no longer knowing how to attain what one seeks.
cases.explication_simple: The ocean of perplexity (ḥayra): he drowns in it, not knowing how to attain the object of his desire, the plaything of the waves that toss him.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The ocean is the ḥayra, perplexity. The desiring soul drowns in the waves of what it does not know how to attain. This square can become an imploring for salvation if it leads one to seek firm ground rather than to love the vertigo.
cases.mecanisme: When figurative desire is not solidly transformed, it leaves the soul in a sea of uncertainty.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that the intensity of the confusion proves the truth of the desire.
remede: Seek firm ground: prayer, counsel, limit.
Do not feed the scenarios that stir the waves.
Ask to be saved from the desire that engulfs.
cases.question_introspection: In which perplexity do I prefer to float rather than return to a shore?
cases.pratique: When desire becomes an ocean, return to the body: walk, breathe, then make a simple request for guidance.
symptomes: Circling around an impossible desire.
Oscillating between hope and discouragement.
Feeling drawn towards the depths by what one pursues.
cases.ouverture: The drowning felt can teach one to seek salvation rather than drunkenness.

cases.resume: Firm ground regained, but still surrounded by threats.
cases.explication_simple: The firm ground he reaches safe and sound — but beasts lie in wait there: no true rest.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The earth represents the return to a support after the drowning. Yet the soul is not yet at peace: symbolic beasts await its weakness. This square teaches that to come out of a danger is not enough; one must also keep vigilance after salvation.
cases.mecanisme: After the fear of drowning, the servant seeks a stable support and returns to the earthly condition.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that touching firm ground means the work is finished.
remede: Consolidate the simple supports.
Do not return too quickly towards the ocean.
Protect the moments of fragility.
cases.question_introspection: Which firm ground must I consolidate before claiming to take to the road again?
cases.pratique: After coming out of a crisis, establish a sober routine of stability: sleep, prayer, order, sound company.
symptomes: Relief after a crisis but unstable vigilance.
Fear that the old danger may return.
The need for a concrete support so as not to fall again.
cases.ouverture: The earth teaches stability, patience and the embodiment of the return.

cases.resume: Fear, which discovers that true fear must be directed towards God.
cases.explication_simple: A voice says to him: "Do not fear them, but fear Me" (Quran III, 175) — fear turns back towards God.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Fear arises before visible threats. But a voice reminds: do not fear them, fear Me if you are believers. Fear then becomes a threshold: it can remain panic before creatures or be purified into the fear of God.
cases.mecanisme: On solid ground, the soul sees the dangers around it and measures its vulnerability.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that all will be well only if circumstances are completely controlled.
remede: Bring multiple fears back to a single noble fear.
Remember that God is with you wherever you are.
Act prudently without worshipping danger.
cases.question_introspection: What do I fear as though it had absolute power over me?
cases.pratique: List your fears, then ask which must become prudence and which must be entrusted to God.
symptomes: Anticipating possible attacks.
Looking everywhere for a threat.
Forgetting that creatures are not sovereign.
cases.ouverture: Fear can become orientation: to fear God frees one from the tyranny of scattered fears.

cases.resume: Reverential awe, which makes one flee everything that is not God.
cases.explication_simple: To fear God and hasten towards Him while fleeing all things. (Variant: the dread, waḥsha, of everything that is not Him.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Khashya is not panic but knowledge of God's rank. It makes the heart shy towards whatever draws it away and makes it hasten towards Him. It transforms fear into sacred orientation.
cases.mecanisme: The fear of creatures is purified by the remembrance of the divine Presence, until it becomes reverential awe and flight towards God.
cases.illusion_possible: To confuse the fear of God with a paralysing religious anguish.
remede: Nourish reverence through knowledge.
Avoid what makes the heart familiar with disobedience.
Pair fear with hope.
cases.question_introspection: Does my fear draw me away from God through anguish, or bring me closer to Him through reverence?
cases.pratique: Before a temptation, do not dramatise: rise inwardly and flee towards God through a simple act.
symptomes: Becoming more delicate before what displeases God.
Feeling an inner distance from distractions.
Seeking refuge in obedience rather than in control.
cases.ouverture: True fear gives movement: it makes one flee towards God, not away from Him.

cases.resume: The in-between, where one sees both outcomes without having yet arrived.
cases.explication_simple: Al-Aʿrāf, between paradise and hell: its inhabitants see both the one and the other. "All will be recognised by their marks" (VII, 45).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Al-Aʿrāf is a place of threshold, between paradise and hell. The traveller sees, recognises, distinguishes; he is no longer in total ignorance, but not yet at the entrance. This square trains one in the humble discernment of consequences.
cases.mecanisme: The fear of God leads to a vantage point where the marks of the paths become visible.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that seeing the outcomes is the same as having chosen the right one.
remede: Do not be content merely to observe.
Ask to enter into what saves.
Choose an act that confirms the orientation.
cases.question_introspection: Where am I still between vision and decision?
cases.pratique: When two paths are clearly visible, perform an act that tips the heart towards the truer way.
symptomes: Seeing good and evil more clearly without being stabilised.
Feeling that one is between two directions.
Fearing to remain a spectator of salvation.
cases.ouverture: The in-between can awaken sincere imploring.

cases.resume: The tongue loosened by God to ask for salvation and good.
cases.explication_simple: When God wishes to give a gift, He loosens the tongue through imploring: "Implore Me, I shall answer you" (XL, 60). (Variant: the loosening of the tongue, ṭalq al-lisān.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Imploring is not only a human initiative: when God wishes to give, He loosens the tongue of His servant. This square teaches one to ask broadly, for this world and the next, for the known good and the unknown, and to seek refuge against the visible and hidden evil.
cases.mecanisme: From the in-between, the servant understands that he cannot cross alone and begins to ask.
cases.illusion_possible: To turn imploring into mere emotion, without sincerity or vigilance.
remede: Ask for the complete good, not only the immediate outcome.
Join the asking to sincerity and recourse.
Keep the adab in the joy that follows invocation.
cases.question_introspection: What do I not dare to ask of God with simplicity?
cases.pratique: Formulate a precise invocation, then remain attentive to the change of conduct that this request demands.
symptomes: A need to plead rather than to master.
Speech that becomes true asking.
Hope that revives after fear.
cases.ouverture: Asking opens the heart to receive what it cannot produce.

cases.resume: The company that corrupts, whether visible or subtle.
cases.explication_simple: He does not know that company is distinguished into good and bad. The worst is hidden: ignorant mystics, hypocritical preachers, heedless tyrants.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Bad company is not only that of the openly disobedient. The text insists on more hidden forms: ignorant mystics, complacent preachers, heedless powerful men. It is dangerous because it shapes judgement and can pull towards blind impulsiveness.
cases.mecanisme: Joy or imploring without discernment can lead towards associations that flatter or lead astray.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that company is good because it speaks of spirituality.
remede: Observe the fruits of a frequentation.
Move away from milieux that diminish the adab.
Seek the company of those who make one better.
cases.question_introspection: Which company nourishes in me what I ought to fight?
cases.pratique: After a meeting, note whether the heart has become more truthful, gentler and more obedient, or more confused.
symptomes: Feeling pulled downwards after certain presences.
Excusing false words because they are seductive.
Adopting the faults of the group one frequents.
cases.ouverture: To discern bad company makes possible the flight towards a healthy solitude or a good company.
cases.chemin_texte: If the servant settles in and takes up residence in bad company, he is seized by the grapnel of blind impulsiveness, which drags him and makes him descend twenty-four degrees, passing through treachery (No. 12), through detestable actions (No. 13) and through hatred (No. 8) to make him fall back into blind impulsiveness, which is the seventh dwelling.

cases.resume: The isolation of the desert after fleeing bad company.
cases.explication_simple: A symbol of his isolation (ʿuzla) from his own people: he becomes morose and takes himself for a fool.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The desert symbolises isolation. It may be necessary in order to leave a corrupting company, but it can also become moroseness, solitary pride or generalised suspicion. This square teaches that solitude must purify, not embitter.
cases.mecanisme: In fleeing bad company, the servant finds himself alone and experiences the aridity of the path.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that all company is bad because one company was.
remede: Keep solitude as a temporary remedy.
Do not judge all servants on the basis of a few wounds.
Ask for a truthful company.
cases.question_introspection: Does my isolation make me freer or more suspicious?
cases.pratique: In a time alone, replace brooding over others with a request for guidance and discernment.
symptomes: Cutting oneself off from everyone out of mistrust.
Confusing solitude with superiority.
Believing oneself foolish or abandoned because one walks alone.
cases.ouverture: The desert can make clearer the need for a true guide and a true company.

cases.resume: The wounded reason that thinks ill of God and of the servants.
cases.explication_simple: Seeing no man of good to frequent, he begins to think ill of God — who, he claims, decreed bad company for him — and of His servants.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Sick reason appears when the desert turns into bitter judgement. The servant no longer sees any good in others and suspects God of having abandoned him to bad company. This square is grave: it can be seized by the grapnel of abasement.
cases.mecanisme: Unpurified isolation distorts the gaze and turns prudence into evil suspicion.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that a bad opinion is proof of intelligence.
remede: Suspend global judgements.
Seek a real sign of goodness in another.
Ask God for a sound and humble reason.
cases.question_introspection: Where does my wound pass itself off as discernment?
cases.pratique: When a thought suspects everything, write the facts separately from the interpretations.
symptomes: Thinking that no one is good.
Interpreting God's decrees as hostile.
Confusing lucidity with cynicism.
cases.ouverture: To heal reason is to recover the good opinion of God and a truer reading of creatures.
cases.chemin_texte: If he halts at this station and settles into it, the grapnel of abasement seizes him and drags him down: he descends twenty-eight abysses, passing in his fall through forbidden things (No. 15) and the great ocean (No. 16), to fall into abasement, which is the fifth dwelling.

cases.resume: Ignorance of God, of His promise and of His threat.
cases.explication_simple: Ignorance of God, of His Promise and of His Threat: he betrays trust, breaks his engagements, transgresses the limits.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Ignorance is not only a lack of information; it becomes a breach of trust, a transgression of limits and a forgetting of consequences. It can be dragged towards the lowest depths. The necessary knowledge is here a protection for the road.
cases.mecanisme: Sick reason closes off access to right knowledge and makes the soul unfaithful to its deposits.
cases.illusion_possible: To take ignorance for spontaneity or freedom.
remede: Learn the necessary foundations of the path.
Return to the known limits before seeking subtleties.
Ask for a knowledge that transforms the act.
cases.question_introspection: What cultivated ignorance allows me not to change?
cases.pratique: Choose an essential question that has been ignored and study it from a reliable source rather than improvising.
symptomes: Acting without considering the promise and the threat.
Breaking the limits on the pretext of not seeing clearly.
Despising the knowledge that would correct the soul.
cases.ouverture: To recognise one's ignorance is already a door towards the request for useful knowledge.
cases.chemin_texte: If he halts and settles into this place, he is seized and dragged by the grapnel of beneath the earth; he descends twenty-eight abysses, passing between hell (No. 14) and forbidden things (No. 15), and falls beneath the earth, which is the sixth dwelling.

cases.resume: The pain before the good given to another.
cases.explication_simple: He envies others the prosperity and health God has given them, and would wish to see them deprived of it.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Envy wishes the favour to be taken away from another because the soul feels deprived. It reveals a contestation of the divine distribution. If one settles into it, it brings one back to perplexity and the fall.
cases.mecanisme: Ignorance of Providence turns the felt deprivation into hostility towards the gifts of others.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that another's good takes something away from one's own destiny.
remede: Bless the good given to another.
Ask God to purify the comparison.
Seek one's own responsibility rather than the disappearance of another's gift.
cases.question_introspection: What good in another awakens in me a contestation of God?
cases.pratique: When envy appears, utter a request for blessing upon the envied person and name your own lack without accusation.
symptomes: Sadness before another's success.
Ceaselessly comparing one's share.
Wanting to diminish what shines in another.
cases.ouverture: Envy can become a knowledge of one's poverty and a request for a just share.
cases.chemin_texte: If he halts and settles into envy, he is seized and dragged by the grapnel of the great ocean, which makes him descend twenty abysses; he passes in his fall through the air (No. 35), through fear (No. 26) and through the earth (No. 25), to fall back into the great ocean, which is the sixteenth dwelling.

cases.resume: The desire to escape towards the sky without being able to fly.
cases.explication_simple: He would like to take flight to escape the painful narrowness of the earth — but he does not know how to fly. (Variant: the sky.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: The air symbolises the impulse to escape from earthly narrowness. But to wish to fly without spiritual wings exposes one to powerlessness. This square teaches one to distinguish aspiration from imaginary flight.
cases.mecanisme: After envy and narrowness, the soul would like to extract itself from its condition rather than pass through it.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that elevation consists in fleeing incarnation.
remede: Return to the possible acts.
Accept the earthly condition without extinguishing the impulse.
Ask for a real, not imaginary, elevation.
cases.question_introspection: Where do I dream of flying in order to avoid walking?
cases.pratique: Transform a dream of escape into a simple action that genuinely elevates the day.
symptomes: Dreaming of a total exit without real means.
Despising ordinary constraints.
Seeking an elevated state so as no longer to feel the earth.
cases.ouverture: Aspiration becomes healthy when it accepts the means and the measure.

cases.resume: Contempt for the world, for oneself, and sometimes for life itself.
cases.explication_simple: He despises the world, despises himself, despises his life to the point of wishing for death — which does not come. (Variant: the mountain.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Contempt appears when the impulse towards the air fails. The soul becomes disgusted with the world and with itself, to the point of wishing to disappear. This square must be read with delicacy: it signals a distress that should lead to the avowal of powerlessness and to rescue, not to isolation.
cases.mecanisme: The inability to rise as one would wish produces bitterness, rejection of the world, rejection of oneself, and the temptation to call detachment what is still distress.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that despising the world amounts to detachment.
remede: Do not remain alone in destructive thoughts.
Distinguish renunciation from hatred of life.
Return to a concrete mercy towards oneself and others.
cases.question_introspection: What do I despise because I do not yet know how to pass through it?
cases.pratique: Name a word of contempt and seek the suffering it protects, then ask for an appropriate rescue.
symptomes: Speaking of everything with contempt.
Judging oneself without mercy.
Confusing lucidity about the world with destructive disgust.
cases.ouverture: Contempt can be passed through if it becomes a recognition of one's real powerlessness.

cases.resume: The avowal of possessing neither strength nor power by oneself.
cases.explication_simple: Unable to free his soul, he recognises his powerlessness and that he possesses no strength of his own — a salutary avowal.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Powerlessness becomes positive when it is no longer despair but truth. The servant recognises that he cannot save his soul by his own strength. This recognition prepares the discovery of his poverty and of the need for God.
cases.mecanisme: Contempt and the failure of escape compel the servant to recognise his limit.
cases.illusion_possible: To confuse spiritual powerlessness with personal worthlessness.
remede: Tell the truth of one's weakness.
Ask for assistance instead of playing the strong one.
Lean on a possible act, not on an imaginary power.
cases.question_introspection: Where must I stop pretending to be able, in order at last to ask for rescue?
cases.pratique: Repeat inwardly: there is no strength and no power except in God, then accomplish the small act asked of you.
symptomes: Ceasing to claim mastery over everything.
Feeling one's dependence without yet living it peacefully.
Being on the verge of a true request.
cases.ouverture: Recognised powerlessness opens onto poverty before God, which is a strength of truth.

cases.resume: The discovery of one's true condition: poverty, dependence, need.
cases.explication_simple: He becomes aware of his own attributes: powerlessness and poverty (faqr). "Become aware of your worth, and He will make you know His own" (Ḥikam).
cases.explication_spirituelle: The thing sought by the servant is not first an external thing: it is the knowledge of his own attributes of poverty and powerlessness. The text cites the idea: know your worth, and He will make you know His own. This square turns the gaze back towards the truth of servanthood.
cases.mecanisme: Accepted powerlessness reveals to the servant what is asked of him: to recognise his own state.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that to know oneself means to contemplate one's qualities rather than to recognise one's poverty.
remede: Meditate on one's limits without contempt.
Render to God the qualities that belong to Him.
Welcome poverty as a door, not as a failure.
cases.question_introspection: What truth of my servanthood opens me to a greater knowledge of God?
cases.pratique: Write three real dependencies in your life and transform them into gratitude or request.
symptomes: Seeing one's dependence more clearly.
Ceasing to claim a greatness that is not one's own.
Beginning to desire a truer relation to God.
cases.ouverture: To know one's poverty makes possible a truer knowledge of Lordship.

cases.resume: Compassion before the manifest indigence of man.
cases.explication_simple: He feels tenderness and pity for the one he sees naked and hungry — the "naked man" designating the essential indigence of man. From there he rises towards good character (57).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Nakedness designates visible indigence. After having known his own poverty, the servant recognises that of another with tenderness. This compassion is a confirmed cause of progression: it can elevate towards good character.
cases.mecanisme: The knowledge of one's own poverty makes one sensitive to the poverty of others; the indigence seen outside awakens the indigence recognised within.
cases.illusion_possible: To use compassion to believe oneself superior to the one one helps.
remede: Help without humiliating.
See the other as a mirror of one's own poverty.
Transform the emotion into concrete kindness.
cases.question_introspection: When I see the indigence of another, is it my heart or my image that responds?
cases.pratique: Perform a discreet gesture towards a visible poverty, without seeking recognition or a story about oneself.
symptomes: Being touched by the vulnerability of another.
No longer regarding weakness only as a fault.
Feeling a gentle responsibility before need.
cases.ouverture: True compassion opens morality because it unites lucidity and mercy.
cases.chemin_texte: If the traveller settles in this station and takes up residence in it, he finds himself pushed towards good character and, in a single step, crosses eighteen stations, passing, in his progression, through the thing sought (No. 38), trouble (No. 43) and real desire (No. 44), to halt at the station of good character (No. 57).

cases.resume: The frequentation that elevates, corrects and protects.
cases.explication_simple: His trials have taught him that he cannot do without good company: "man will be resurrected in the religion of his friend". It elevates him to good actions (64).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Good company is a pivot of the Chessboard. The earlier experiences teach that one does not journey alone and that characters are transmitted. It elevates to good works, but can also become a threshold where one refuses the perfect guide out of a sense of having already arrived.
cases.mecanisme: After having discerned bad company and his own poverty, the servant seeks a presence that orients him towards the good.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that good company replaces the guide, the Law or personal work.
remede: Choose presences by their fruits.
Remain teachable even when one progresses.
Do not despise the spiritual intermediary.
cases.question_introspection: With whom does my soul become more obedient, more humble and more alive?
cases.pratique: Name a company that elevates you and one that lowers you, then concretely adjust the time given to each.
symptomes: Feeling truer beside certain people.
Receiving a correction without being destroyed.
Wanting to practise the good by luminous contagion.
cases.ouverture: Good company shows that the soul also heals through proximity with the truthful.
cases.chemin_texte: He thus joins good company along the way, and if he settles with it, it elevates him to good actions (No. 64), which makes him cross twenty-four stations in a single step.

cases.resume: The faithful preservation of the senses and the deposits.
cases.explication_simple: To preserve one's external and internal senses from falling into what the Law declares forbidden (muḥarram) or detestable (makrūh).
cases.explication_spirituelle: The pleasing trust consists in protecting the external and internal senses from what is forbidden or detestable. It is born within good company and makes the presence reliable. The traveller learns that faithfulness is also at stake in what the eyes, the ears, the imagination and the heart receive.
cases.mecanisme: Good company educates the guarding of the visible and invisible deposits, until the servant becomes attentive to what his senses receive.
cases.illusion_possible: To reduce faithfulness to external honesty while neglecting the inner senses.
remede: Guard the senses as entrusted doors.
Avoid what soils the imagination.
Honour the small faithfulnesses.
cases.question_introspection: What sense have I left unguarded though it was entrusted to me?
cases.pratique: Choose one sensory door to guard today: gaze, hearing, speech or imagination.
symptomes: Watching over what one looks at, listens to and nourishes.
Feeling that a small lapse weakens the trust.
Desiring to be reliable before God even without a witness.
cases.ouverture: Faithfulness makes the heart habitable for fine inspirations.

cases.resume: The subtle call that comforts and orients the soul.
cases.explication_simple: A lordly voice (hātif rabbānī) speaking through the tongue of the Spirit: the soul finds it sweet and wishes to conform to its announcement, without its own strength helping it to do so.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The subtle voice is described as lordly, speaking through the tongue of the Spirit. It gladdens the soul and calls it to a more perfect conformity. But it also reveals the weakness of the body and of human strengths: the call is greater than what one can accomplish alone.
cases.mecanisme: The faithfulness of the senses makes the soul more apt to receive a fine inner announcement.
cases.illusion_possible: To appropriate every subtle inspiration as though it guaranteed one's sainthood.
remede: Test the inspiration by the Law, the adab and sobriety.
Do not publish too quickly what is received.
Ask for the strength to obey the call.
cases.question_introspection: What inner voice calls me to the good, and how do I avoid appropriating it?
cases.pratique: When a luminous intuition arrives, note it soberly, then seek what humble act it asks for.
symptomes: A luminous intuition that encourages the good.
A delicate joy before an inner word.
Feeling at once the call and one's inability to respond to it perfectly.
cases.ouverture: The subtle call awakens without removing the need for humility and rescue.

cases.resume: The trouble of the soul caught between aspiration and the limits of the world.
cases.explication_simple: The troubled soul suffers from the grip of the senses and the difficulties of the world — death, illness, poverty, trial, separation: "a mine of troubles, that you may be incited to renunciation" (Ḥikam).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Trouble comes from the human condition: a failing body, a failing heart, a world made of illness, separation, death and difficulties. The text recalls that this dwelling naturally produces troubles in order to incite renunciation. This square does not condemn trouble; it interprets it as a call to leave attachment.
cases.mecanisme: The subtle call meets the thickness of the body, the senses and the trials of this world.
cases.illusion_possible: To be astonished that the world troubles, as if it ought to produce a definitive peace.
remede: Accept that this dwelling is a mine of troubles.
Do not confuse trouble with abandonment by God.
Orient the aspiration towards the Real.
cases.question_introspection: What trouble pushes me to release what I wanted to take as a dwelling?
cases.pratique: Facing a trouble, ask: what does it teach me about the nature of this world and about my attachment?
symptomes: Wanting a pure action with still-mixed means.
Being gripped by the cares of the human condition.
Feeling that the world cannot give what it promises.
cases.ouverture: Trouble can become lucid renunciation and aspiration towards the world of the Spirit.

cases.resume: The desire for God, born beyond forms and consolations.
cases.explication_simple: The desire felt by souls and spirits for their Creator: "Love God for the favours with which He nourishes you". The heart has but one direction: turned towards Him, it draws away from all else.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Real desire is the love of souls and spirits for their Creator. It receives created beauties as signs, but does not stop at them. It becomes a cause of spiritual jealousy, of ardent longing and of deep love.
cases.mecanisme: The trouble of the world and the purified figurative desire orient the heart towards the One who gives all favours.
cases.illusion_possible: To use the language of divine love to cover an attachment that is still figurative.
remede: Refer every love back to God.
Protect love by prayer and the adab.
Do not confuse affective intensity with the truth of the orientation.
cases.question_introspection: Does my love make me freer for God or more captive to a form?
cases.pratique: When a love passes through you, seek what it reveals of the desire for God and what purification it demands.
symptomes: A desire for God stronger than the possession of forms.
Dissatisfaction before consolations that are merely created.
A love that seeks prayer, proximity and presence.
cases.ouverture: Real desire gives the heart its deep direction.

cases.resume: The ruins of a misunderstood detachment.
cases.explication_simple: He takes everything in aversion and breaks his worldly ties; but if he halts there, he ruins his temporal world — which is nonetheless the foundation of his way. (Variant: the calamity, al-wabāl.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: The ruins appear when the servant outwardly rejects the lawful means because he wishes to detach himself inwardly. The text warns: to ruin one's temporal world can corrupt the character and become a fall. Detachment is not irresponsible destruction.
cases.mecanisme: Real desire, misunderstood, can make one take in aversion all that occupies one away from God.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that using lawful means contradicts the detachment of the heart.
remede: Do not destroy what God has established as a means.
Seek counsel before radical ruptures.
Re-establish the concrete duties neglected.
cases.question_introspection: What do I risk ruining by calling it detachment?
cases.pratique: Examine a contemplated renunciation: does it come from God, from wisdom, or from a weariness disguised as spirituality?
symptomes: Abandoning responsibilities in the name of the spiritual.
Confusing the breaking of worldly ties with the neglect of means.
Becoming embittered after having destroyed one's supports.
cases.ouverture: The ruins teach that one must hold together inner surrender and outer wisdom.
cases.chemin_texte: He falls into indigence, his breast tightens, his temperament sours; the grapnel of bad character (No. 18) seizes him and drags him down: from the ruins, he descends thirty-three steps, passing through contempt (No. 36), powerlessness (No. 37), the ocean (No. 24), figurative desire (No. 23), to fall into bad character, which is the eighteenth dwelling.

cases.resume: The effacement where even the awareness of effacement disappears.
cases.explication_simple: Surrender (tawakkul) to God: the universes are effaced from his heart — no existence, no usefulness, no harm — and the very awareness of this effacement is effaced.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Effacement within effacement designates trusting surrender. The universes are effaced from the heart as independent sources of profit or harm, then the soul ceases even to attribute this effacement to itself. It is a grace, not a performance.
cases.mecanisme: After the danger of the ruins, detachment is rectified into inner tawakkul rather than outer destruction.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe oneself the author of one's effacement and to make of it a new spiritual self.
remede: Render the state to God.
Do not despise the visible means.
Keep gratitude and discretion.
cases.question_introspection: Even in my detachment, what do I continue to attribute to myself?
cases.pratique: Before a cause that impresses you, say: it is a means, not a source; then act justly with it.
symptomes: Seeing creatures without giving them absolute power.
Ceasing to dramatise gain and loss.
Recognising that even detachment is given.
cases.ouverture: Effacement frees the heart from causes while leaving the body to act with wisdom.

cases.resume: The intelligence that puts each thing in its place before God.
cases.explication_simple: That which grasps the divine enunciation and puts everything in its place: the universes are affirmed by the Affirmation of God and effaced by the Unity of His Essence. (Variant: adherence, taḥqīq.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Perfect intelligence understands the divine enunciation as God approves it. It affirms the causes because God affirms them, but removes from them all independent influence. It reconciles the law of the heart, which is surrender, and that of the body, which is the use of means.
cases.mecanisme: True effacement leads to a balanced understanding of the causes, of Wisdom and of Power.
cases.illusion_possible: To take surrender to God for the refusal of means, or the means for autonomous powers.
remede: Employ the lawful means.
Attribute the effect to God alone.
Verify that the thought leads to obedience and not to discourse.
cases.question_introspection: Where do I deny the causes, or on the contrary attribute too much to them?
cases.pratique: In an important action, distinguish: what means must I employ, and to whom must I entrust the result?
symptomes: Acting with the means without relying on them ultimately.
Not opposing spirituality and responsibility.
Seeing the rights of God in the movements of the soul.
cases.ouverture: Perfect intelligence gives a profound sobriety: neither fatalism, nor idolatry of causes.

cases.resume: The realisations that recognise the limits of thought.
cases.explication_simple: He realises that perfect intelligence has, for its lowest degree, the abandonment of the world, and for its highest, the abandonment of reflection upon the Essence: God is beyond the reach of thought, which He created. He aspires to know God through God.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The realisations lead the intelligence to its threshold: God cannot be grasped by a created thought. The servant then aspires to a knowledge coming from God and through God. To halt there can elevate him towards the supreme world.
cases.mecanisme: Perfect intelligence leads to the realisation that thought cannot reach the Essence.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that to realise the limits of thought authorises despising knowledge.
remede: Keep knowledge in its place.
Ask for a useful and transforming knowledge.
Do not speak beyond what is given.
cases.question_introspection: What truth do I understand mentally without yet receiving it from God?
cases.pratique: Take a cherished spiritual idea and ask: has it become transformation, or only mental possession?
symptomes: Understanding the limits of speculation.
Desiring a received knowledge, not a fabricated one.
Feeling that concepts must bow before the Real.
cases.ouverture: Realisation makes knowledge humble and available to a higher knowledge.

cases.resume: Sincere nostalgia before the gap between inner realisation and incarnate life.
cases.explication_simple: The sadness of the sincere: returned to his body, he no longer finds, outwardly, the proof of what he has realised; his heart fills with nostalgia. His remedy: to entrust soul and goods to the Most High.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The nostalgic heart returns to the body and does not yet find, in its outward manifestation, the proof of what it has understood. This sadness seeks a remedy: to entrust to God one's soul and one's goods. It prepares the consecration in the way of God.
cases.mecanisme: After a realisation, the servant feels the gap between what has been seen and what is lived.
cases.illusion_possible: To indulge in nostalgia instead of concretely offering one's life.
remede: Entrust to God one's soul, one's goods, one's prayer, one's life and one's death.
Seek incarnate coherence.
Make of sadness an active offering.
cases.question_introspection: Where has my inner understanding not yet joined my real life?
cases.pratique: Name a domain of life not entrusted to God and perform there an act of concrete consecration.
symptomes: Sadness at not embodying what has been understood.
A desire to entrust one's whole life to God.
Nostalgia for a more total coherence.
cases.ouverture: Nostalgia becomes sincere when it leads to the gift of self.

cases.resume: Energy and goods consecrated to the way of God.
cases.explication_simple: He spends energy and goods in the way of God: everything that befalls him, in good or in ill, is integrated into it — "you are but a bleeding finger".
cases.explication_spirituelle: To be in the way of God means that what happens, good or ill, is integrated into an orientation. The servant spends his energy and his goods for God. But this station remains exposed: visible effort can slide towards false devotion.
cases.mecanisme: Sincere nostalgia is converted into the spending of self for God: the heart no longer wishes only to understand, it wishes to commit its strength and its goods.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that the intensity of commitment automatically protects from the ego.
remede: Purify the intention before the action.
Do not seek to be seen as committed.
Accept the small hidden works.
cases.question_introspection: Does what I call the way of God seek God or my own importance?
cases.pratique: Before a useful action, formulate the intention and accept that no one may recognise it.
symptomes: Placing one's strength at the service of a just cause.
Reading wounds and difficulties within an orientation towards God.
Giving of one's time, one's goods, one's person.
cases.ouverture: The way of God unifies events around an intention.

cases.resume: The devotion that wants to be seen, heard or admired.
cases.explication_simple: Acting or refraining in order to be seen and to earn praise. If others do not respond, he grows resentful — the grapnel of hatred seizes him.
cases.explication_spirituelle: False devotion arises even after one has committed to the path. The soul multiplies acts in order to attract praise and builds a pious identity. This square is one of the great traps: the religious act becomes food for the self.
cases.mecanisme: Effort on the path, if it is not purified after the action, seeks the regard of men and turns devotion into a staging of the self.
cases.illusion_possible: Believing that the quantity of devotional acts cancels the need for sincerity.
remede: Conceal certain acts of good.
Ask for sincerity before and after the action.
Accept being forgotten by men.
cases.question_introspection: What good do I do less for God than to be confirmed?
cases.pratique: Choose a good deed that will remain entirely unknown and observe the resistance of the self.
symptomes: Subtly letting one's practices be known.
Being hurt when the good done is not recognised.
Preferring visible acts to hidden faithfulness.
cases.ouverture: False devotion unveiled can lead back to secret worship.

cases.resume: The humble dust, trodden by all, able to turn refuse into fruit.
cases.explication_simple: Everything appears to him as dust, and he sees himself as dust: trodden by all, it turns refuse into flowers and fruits.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Dust is a major image of humility. It claims nothing, receives the steps of all, and transforms what is thrown upon it. Here the servant learns not to defend himself through importance, but to become a place of humble fruitfulness.
cases.mecanisme: False devotion can be corrected by seeing oneself as dust: the soul learns to lose the need to be placed above others.
cases.illusion_possible: Playing at humility in order to be admired as humble.
remede: Serve without status.
Receive vexations without losing one's dignity.
Turn refuse into useful good.
cases.question_introspection: Can I be useful without being recognised as important?
cases.pratique: Carry out a humble, unseen task, and do it like earth that bears fruit without a sound.
symptomes: Accepting to be little regarded.
No longer measuring one's worth by the regard received.
Turning a humiliation into fertility.
cases.ouverture: Dust teaches a fruitful humility, not a sterile humiliation.

cases.resume: The water that gives life effortlessly and infuses itself into things.
cases.explication_simple: The water through which life is given to all things, which infuses itself and brings life without pain or effort.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Water gives life without a sound. After dust, it represents a gentle, penetrating, non-possessive usefulness. The servant becomes beneficial not through tension, but through a living presence.
cases.mecanisme: The humility of dust allows a more fluid fruitfulness, like water that gives life.
cases.illusion_possible: Believing that the gentleness of water means the absence of limits or form.
remede: Remain supple without dissolving.
Give life rather than prove.
Seek purity of intention.
cases.question_introspection: Does my presence give life, or does it seek to dominate?
cases.pratique: Today, help someone in a fluid way, without controlling the outcome or demanding gratitude.
symptomes: A presence that soothes and gives life.
Helping without forcing.
A suppleness that meets situations without losing its nature.
cases.ouverture: Water teaches discreet usefulness and circulating mercy.

cases.resume: The rest of one who is no longer weary of himself nor of others.
cases.explication_simple: Useful to all beings, he is no longer weary of himself nor of others, and he wishes them all the good he desires for himself. (Variant: resignation, al-jalad.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Rest is not idleness but deliverance from the exhaustion of self. The traveller becomes useful to beings because he wishes them the good he desires for himself. This peace prepares courage.
cases.mecanisme: Life-giving water leads to a less strained relationship with oneself and with creatures; usefulness no longer comes from tension but from a heart at rest.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing spiritual rest with a comfortable withdrawal from all responsibility.
remede: Taste rest without falling asleep in it.
Wish for others the good wished for oneself.
Let peace become service.
cases.question_introspection: Of what am I weary: of real service, or of my own self?
cases.pratique: In a wearying relationship, ask God for a rest of the heart that allows one to wish good without tension.
symptomes: Less weariness in bearing others.
A more natural benevolence.
The capacity to wish good without calculation.
cases.ouverture: Rest frees one from obsession with self and prepares a more courageous commitment.

cases.resume: The courage that exposes its goods and its person for the truth.
cases.explication_simple: Courage in every sense: he heeds no dangers and does not fear to pay with his person and his goods. From here he rises to the testimonial vision (90).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Courage here is broad: moral, scientific, political, martial, existential. It does not fear dangers when the divine Word and the truth are at stake. To settle in it can raise one to the testimonial vision.
cases.mecanisme: Rest frees the energy needed to face difficulties without excessive fear.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing courage with a taste for danger or for the heroic image.
remede: Check that courage serves God, not the ego.
Keep prudence and intention.
Accept proportionate sacrifices.
cases.question_introspection: Where is my prudence wisdom, and where is it fear in disguise?
cases.pratique: Identify a truth or a good that calls for concrete courage, then take one measured step.
symptomes: Speaking truly when it would be more comfortable to keep silent.
Paying with one's person for the good.
Not fleeing necessary risks.
cases.ouverture: Courage places strength at the service of an orientation higher than the survival of the self.

cases.resume: The social beauty of courage, exposed to regard and admiration.
cases.explication_simple: His civic and scientific courage becomes an adornment that beautifies him in the eyes of rivals and kings — a beauty that can hold one back.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Adornment appears when courage becomes an ornament in the eyes of others. It may be legitimate as the fruit of a nobility, but it carries the risk of image. The soul loves to be beautiful with its virtues.
cases.mecanisme: Visible courage attracts esteem and gives the person an honourable form; what was service can then become a beloved adornment.
cases.illusion_possible: Believing that the radiance of a virtue guarantees its purity.
remede: Render the radiance back to the One who gives.
Conceal part of the good accomplished.
Not confuse reputation with reality.
cases.question_introspection: Which virtue do I love to wear as an adornment?
cases.pratique: When a quality is recognised, respond inwardly with gratitude and ask for protection against appropriation.
symptomes: Feeling beautified by one's commitment.
Loving to be recognised as courageous or noble.
Slipping from service into posture.
cases.ouverture: Adornment can become gratitude if the beauty is rendered back to God.

cases.resume: Good morality that honours and avoids coarseness.
cases.explication_simple: He honours those who honour him and avoids coarse people (those whose morals are not refined by pure worship).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Good character is a practical summit: in relationship it manifests what the heart has learned. It honours those who honour and avoids coarseness without letting itself be contaminated. It is also a confirmed ascent from compassion.
cases.mecanisme: Compassion, good company and purified adornment lead to a nobler conduct.
cases.illusion_possible: Reducing good character to an outward politeness without purification of the heart.
remede: Practise honour without flattery.
Avoid the coarse without hatred.
Correct speech before it wounds.
cases.question_introspection: Does my character bear witness to what I claim to seek?
cases.pratique: Choose a daily interaction and deliberately bring to it honour, gentleness and a just limit.
symptomes: Delicacy in speech.
The capacity to avoid coarseness without contempt.
Active respect towards those who manifest the good.
cases.ouverture: Good morality makes an inner transformation visible.

cases.resume: Reflection on the inner aspect of things and their consequences.
cases.explication_simple: The thinking brain: it reflects on the inner aspect of things and observes that the useful and the harmful are in the Hand of God alone.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The thinking brain is not dry intellectualism. It meditates on the inner aspect of things and sees that the useful and the harmful are in the Hand of God. Thought becomes an instrument for discerning consequences.
cases.mecanisme: Good character gives enough stability to reflect on things without a coarse reaction.
