cases.illusion_possible: Believing that thinking rightly is enough, without loving and acting rightly.
remede: Think through the consequences before acting.
Not separate reflection from invocation.
Ask that intelligence serve the heart.
cases.question_introspection: Does my thinking light my path, or does it protect me from commitment?
cases.pratique: Before a decision, look at its consequences on the heart, on others, and on the relationship with God.
symptomes: Examining the aftermath of an act.
Seeking the inner meaning behind appearance.
Referring profit and harm back to God.
cases.ouverture: Thought becomes luminous when it leads to love for God.

cases.resume: Love of all beings for God.
cases.explication_simple: He loves all beings for God: the sincere friend guides him, the enemy follows his trail, but his love never ceases to grow firmer. (The word in the text is ḥubb; "mahabba" on the board is a printing error.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Love is born of the vision that the useful and the harmful are in the Hand of God. The sincere friend guides towards the good; even the enemy can reveal something of the path. Love becomes broader than personal preference.
cases.mecanisme: Reflection on realities and their consequences opens onto a love referred back to God.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing love for God with the absence of discernment.
remede: Love for God, not for one's own benefit.
Keep the necessary limits.
Ask for a heart broad and lucid.
cases.question_introspection: Is my love dependence, preference, or orientation towards God?
cases.pratique: Before a difficult person, ask to see what love for God requires: closeness, limit, forgiveness or silence.
symptomes: Loving without wanting to possess.
Seeing the possible good in beings.
Not letting hostility suppress inner mercy.
cases.ouverture: Purified love widens the heart without abolishing the truth.

cases.resume: The fire born of the intensity of love and the desire to meet.
cases.explication_simple: The fire born of the intensity of love (mahabba) and of the desire to meet God and His Satisfaction.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Fire here is no longer merely anger: it is the burning of love and the desire to meet God and His Satisfaction. But all intensity must be educated, otherwise it can consume without enlightening.
cases.mecanisme: Love, in intensifying, becomes heat, aspiration and inner burning; this ardour calls for an orientation so as not to consume.
cases.illusion_possible: Taking all intensity for a proof of spiritual truth.
remede: Channel ardour through obedience.
Keep measure and gentleness.
Not burn others with one's own fire.
cases.question_introspection: Does the fire that dwells in me enlighten, warm, or consume?
cases.pratique: Transform an ardour into service, prayer or concrete patience, rather than into burning speech.
symptomes: Ardour in the desire for God.
Impatience to meet what is loved.
Inner energy difficult to contain.
cases.ouverture: Purified fire gives strength, fervour and movement.

cases.resume: The powerful gentleness towards one who acts wrongly.
cases.explication_simple: He acts with benevolence towards whoever acts wrongly, restores ties with whoever has broken them — which pushes him towards the perfect guide (62).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Clemency answers evil with an active nobility: benevolence towards the offender, renewal of ties with the one who breaks them. It does not deny the wrong, but refuses to let the wrong dictate the quality of the heart.
cases.mecanisme: The fire of love, if purified, becomes a capacity for patience and kindness towards offence.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing clemency with the absence of limits before injustice.
remede: Delay the riposte.
Distinguish inner forgiveness from outer limit.
Restore what can be restored without feeding the abuse.
cases.question_introspection: Where can I be firm without losing clemency?
cases.pratique: Faced with an offence, choose a response that protects dignity without imitating the wrong received.
symptomes: Responding less quickly to provocation.
Keeping a kindness that is not weakness.
Seeking reconciliation when it is just.
cases.ouverture: Clemency manifests a strength greater than reaction.

cases.resume: The living master who knows the road, its perils and its returns.
cases.explication_simple: The living master who welcomes him: "Be welcome, I have long awaited you," and invites him to enter the Presence of God. In a single step he leads him to permanence in God (96).
cases.explication_spirituelle: The perfect guide is a central station. He welcomes, reassures, announces a possibility of permanence in God, then teaches the adab of the dangerous stations. The source insists: there is no better remedy for sick hearts than the company of the knowers under a just education.
cases.mecanisme: Clemency and maturity of heart make the servant able to hear the call of a true guide.
cases.illusion_possible: Believing one can do without any intermediary because one has tasted a few states.
remede: Seek truthfulness, knowledge, adab and sobriety in the guide.
Remain teachable.
Not idolise the person: follow the guidance towards God.
cases.question_introspection: Am I truly guidable, or only interested in the idea of a guide?
cases.pratique: Reconsider one's relationship to accompaniment: who makes me more humble, more upright and more attached to God?
symptomes: Recognising the need to be led.
Receiving a word that protects and orients.
Sometimes resisting out of unworthiness or out of a feeling of being already higher.
cases.ouverture: The guide protects from the confusions that the soul cannot always see alone.

cases.resume: The conviction of one's own perfection, subtle and dangerous.
cases.explication_simple: The guide's word "you are worthy of it": a conviction that forms a veil between good company (40) and the perfect guide (62).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Self-conviction appears when the servant believes himself equal or superior to the guide, sometimes under the pretext of scruple. It may seem pure, but it veils the usefulness of the intermediary and feeds self-sufficiency.
cases.mecanisme: Spiritual experience without submission to a guidance can become certainty of self.
cases.illusion_possible: Taking one's own conviction for a divine authorisation.
remede: Submit one's certainties to a reliable outer verification.
Remember the dangers of the solitary journey.
Ask for protection against admiration of one's own opinion.
cases.question_introspection: Which personal certainty makes me deaf to guidance?
cases.pratique: Choose a strong conviction and ask: who could legitimately correct me on this point?
symptomes: Believing oneself advanced enough no longer to listen.
Interpreting one's autonomy as superior probity.
Receiving warnings as useless.
cases.ouverture: Seeing this conviction as a veil can give the soul back the humility to learn.

cases.resume: The good works that embody faith in service.
cases.explication_simple: Good works: fasting, night prayer, protection of the widow and the orphan.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Good works include prayer, fasting, vigil, protection of the weak. They are fruits of good company, but they can remain incomplete if they do not lead to certainty and sincerity. They train the body to take part in the path.
cases.mecanisme: Good company and moral impetus translate into useful and pious acts; transformation then begins to take shape in works.
cases.illusion_possible: Believing that the accumulation of works is enough without purification of intention.
remede: Join action to sincerity.
Prefer constancy to brilliance.
Link each work to God, not to one's self-image.
cases.question_introspection: Which good work must my body learn to carry with constancy?
cases.pratique: Choose a regular good work, modest and lasting, rather than a great passing impulse.
symptomes: Desire to accomplish concrete acts of good.
Service of the vulnerable.
Regularity in practices that order one's life.
cases.ouverture: Good works give the path a visible and bodily reality.

cases.resume: The certainty founded on the concordance of revealed truths and their signs.
cases.explication_simple: The knowledge of certainty (ʿilm al-yaqīn): he observes the concordance between revealed truths and their convincing signs.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Didactic certainty is a knowledge of certainty. It sees the agreement between the teachings received and their proofs. It strengthens, but it can remain at the level of knowledge if it does not become taste and march.
cases.mecanisme: Good works and transmitted knowledge produce a stability of belief in which revealed truths are confirmed by their signs.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing taught certainty with lived realisation.
remede: Bring certainty down into the act.
Not despise those who still walk in doubt.
Ask for the passage from knowledge to taste.
cases.question_introspection: Which certainty remains in me an idea rather than a way of living?
cases.pratique: Take one certain truth and ask how it concretely changes my day.
symptomes: Feeling the religious truths hold together.
Being reassured by the convincing signs.
Relying on a clear doctrine.
cases.ouverture: Certainty gives a basis for advancing without being tossed about by every doubt.

cases.resume: The contemplation of higher realities through clairvoyance.
cases.explication_simple: The supreme world, "the eye of certainty" (ʿayn al-yaqīn): through his clairvoyance he contemplates the angels, paradise, hell and the traditional realities. (Variant: useful knowledge.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: The supreme world is linked to the eye of certainty and to useful knowledge. The servant contemplates angels, paradise, hell and the traditional realities. But every higher vision must remain ordered, otherwise it can feed fascination or superiority.
cases.mecanisme: Realisations or certainty open onto a higher perception of the invisible realities.
cases.illusion_possible: Seeking the supreme world for the extraordinary rather than for obedience.
remede: Return to the obligations and to adab.
Not run after visions.
Measure all knowledge by its fruits of uprightness.
cases.question_introspection: Does what I seek of the higher world make me more of a servant?
cases.pratique: When an invisible reality fascinates, ask what humble act it calls for in the visible.
symptomes: A deep interest in the invisible worlds.
Clairvoyance of spiritual consequences.
The sense that the visible world does not exhaust the real.
cases.ouverture: Seeing higher must make one more humble, more responsible and more attached to God.

cases.resume: Good pleasure: being satisfied with God, with islam, with the Prophet and with the decrees.
cases.explication_simple: Divine good pleasure (Riḍwān), a station crossed on the way to Paradise and to the testimonial vision.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Riḍwān is a deep satisfaction: accepting God as Lord, islam as religion, Muḥammad as prophet, and the divine decrees in what does or does not depend on responsibility. This station gives an active peace, not a dead resignation.
cases.mecanisme: The vision of the higher realities strengthens satisfaction with the divine order.
cases.illusion_possible: Calling satisfaction what is only passivity or the absence of courage.
remede: Distinguish the decree endured from the duty to be accomplished.
Renew the acceptance of God as Lord.
Transform satisfaction into obedience.
cases.question_introspection: Which decree do I still resist as if I knew better than God?
cases.pratique: Faced with an unchosen event, say yes to the decree without abandoning what must be done.
symptomes: Less inner contestation.
A deeper consent to the path received.
Peace before what escapes the will.
cases.ouverture: Good pleasure frees energy to struggle where responsibility remains.

cases.resume: The combat against the soul and the rigorous examination of one's conduct.
cases.explication_simple: The combat — the lesser and the greater holy war — a station crossed towards the testimonial vision.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Jihād here is inner combat and examination of conscience: calling the soul to account, obliging it to return to what pleases God. It is born of good pleasure, not of rage. This struggle prepares useful knowledge.
cases.mecanisme: True satisfaction gives the strength to combat what in oneself resists God; consent to the decree becomes a demand upon the soul.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing spiritual combat with harshness towards oneself or towards others.
remede: Practise muḥāsaba without despair.
Choose one precise combat.
Join firmness with mercy.
cases.question_introspection: Which inner front calls today for a just and measured combat?
cases.pratique: In the evening, examine one action: intention, conformity, effect on the heart, correction for tomorrow.
symptomes: Examining oneself without complacency.
Bringing the soul back to the divine arbitration.
Struggling against the habits that contradict faith.
cases.ouverture: Just combat frees the soul from its tyrannies and makes it available to useful knowledge.

cases.resume: The knowledge of the heart that becomes useful because it transforms.
cases.explication_simple: Useful knowledge, that of the heart (and not of the tongue alone): "whoever acts according to what he knows, God makes him know what he did not know." From here, the ascent towards the Muḥammadan kingdom (94).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Useful knowledge is distinguished from the knowledge of the tongue. It is not a mere argument against man; it becomes light of the heart. To settle in it raises one towards the Muḥammadan kingdom.
cases.mecanisme: Combat and sincere examination make knowledge necessary, practical and inward.
cases.illusion_possible: Taking an abundance of information for useful knowledge.
remede: Seek the knowledge that corrects the act.
Put into practice what is learned.
Ask for protection against the knowledge that argues against oneself.
cases.question_introspection: Which knowledge will become an argument against me if I do not practise it?
cases.pratique: After each spiritual learning, write down one concrete consequence to practise within twenty-four hours.
symptomes: Learning in order to be transformed.
Feeling that a knowledge obliges.
Preferring the knowledge that purifies to the one that impresses.
cases.ouverture: Useful knowledge links knowledge, faith, path and Muḥammadan servanthood.
cases.chemin_texte: If he settles in this station and dwells there, he is raised towards the Muḥammadan kingdom (No. 94) and crosses in a single step twenty-five degrees, passing through the stations of the way (No. 72), of the sublime dust (No. 73), of the kingdom (No. 88), of holiness (No. 87) before arriving at the Muḥammadan kingdom.

cases.resume: The intimate faith that loves what God loves and advances according to its strength.
cases.explication_simple: Perfect and intimate faith (ghaybī) in God, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day and predestination: he loves what God loves and detests what He detests.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Perfect and intimate faith comprises the pillars of belief and the adherence of the heart. It can appear when the traveller does not yet have the strength to follow the prophetic traces fully, but seeks the means to attain it.
cases.mecanisme: Useful knowledge leads to a deeper adherence to the realities of faith, even when the strength to walk fully is still lacking.
cases.illusion_possible: Contenting oneself with believing without seeking the means to live the faith.
remede: Nourish faith through knowledge and act.
Seek the means suited to one's weakness.
Not turn powerlessness into a permanent excuse.
cases.question_introspection: Does my faith remain inner assent, or does it become the orientation of my choices?
cases.pratique: Choose one aspect of faith and translate it today into a visible behaviour.
symptomes: Believing beyond the visible.
Loving what God loves, detesting what He detests.
Walking according to the measure of one's weakness without abandoning aspiration.
cases.ouverture: Intimate faith gives a direction even when strength is still lacking.

cases.resume: The prophetic word as the outer form of the way.
cases.explication_simple: The religious Law (sharīʿa), which is none other than the words of the Prophet: he learns it and respects it with a new zeal, born of faith having entered the heart.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The religious Law is not opposed to depth: it gives the heart a form, a measure and a protection. The servant learns it with zeal because faith has entered his heart. It prevents the spiritual impulse from becoming imagination without rule.
cases.mecanisme: Intimate faith seeks a normative form in the words of the Prophet; the heart no longer wishes to believe without learning what is asked of it.
cases.illusion_possible: Opposing the Law to inner reality, or reducing the Law to a shell without a heart.
remede: Learn the necessary obligations.
Practise without harshness or pride.
Link the rule to mercy and to truth.
cases.question_introspection: Which prophetic form is still lacking to my inner aspiration?
cases.pratique: Take up a rule that is known but neglected and practise it with intention, not as an automatism.
symptomes: Desire to learn what is asked.
A more living respect for the limits.
Understanding that love needs a form.
cases.ouverture: The Law protects the way and gives the body an obedience.

cases.resume: The prophetic acts as a lived way.
cases.explication_simple: The inner way (ṭarīqa), a station crossed towards the testimonial vision.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The Muḥammadan ṭarīqa is here the acts of the Prophet. Having learned the normative word, the servant tastes the sweetness of acting in conformity. The way becomes a living imitation, not a mere idea.
cases.mecanisme: The Law learned pushes towards enactment and walking in the prophetic traces.
cases.illusion_possible: Speaking of the way without accepting the concrete discipline of acts.
remede: Choose a sunna or a prophetic act to embody soberly.
Avoid ostentation in imitation.
Link each act to love of the Prophet and of God.
cases.question_introspection: Which part of my life speaks of the way without yet walking?
cases.pratique: Take a daily gesture and reshape it according to prophetic nobility: speech, meal, relationship, rest.
symptomes: Seeking how to act, not only what to believe.
Tasting a sweetness in conformity.
Feeling that the way passes through ordinary gestures.
cases.ouverture: The way unifies knowledge, body, heart and prophetic imitation.

cases.resume: The threshold of the sublime reward, antechamber of paradise.
cases.explication_simple: The sublime dust: a high station crossed on the way to the testimonial vision (90).
cases.explication_spirituelle: The sublime dust marks the approach of the blessed dwelling promised to the servants who act according to their knowledge. It is a reward, but also a threshold: the soul must still learn not to stop at the gift.
cases.mecanisme: The way practised opens towards reward and the hope of paradise; the servant approaches a fruit without having to stop at the fruit.
cases.illusion_possible: Stopping at the reward while forgetting the One who rewards.
remede: Receive hope with gratitude.
Not worship the reward.
Ask for God beyond His gifts.
cases.question_introspection: Do I seek the gift, or the One who gives?
cases.pratique: Before a spiritual consolation, give thanks and then ask not to stop at the consolation.
symptomes: Feeling the nearness of a spiritual fruit.
Hoping for the promised reward.
Being encouraged by the signs of the good.
cases.ouverture: The sublime reward encourages, but it must orient further towards the divine Face.

cases.resume: Paradise seen as reward, still insufficient for the desire of the Face.
cases.explication_simple: Paradise: he sees its splendour, the houris, the palaces as gifts — but he remembers the warning of the perfect guide.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Paradise appears with its splendour, but the traveller remembers that his ultimate aspiration is the vision of God. He renounces stopping at the reward in order to desire the lifting of the veil. This square distinguishes the beatitude received from the quest for the Giver.
cases.mecanisme: The sublime reward introduces the vision of paradise and its delights, then reveals that the desire for God exceeds even the gift received.
cases.illusion_possible: Believing that the reward is enough to extinguish the desire for God.
remede: Honour the reward without absolutising it.
Ask for the lifting of the veil.
Not despise paradise, but not make it the end of love.
cases.question_introspection: Which reward could make me forget the face of the Giver?
cases.pratique: In a consolation, ask: what in me still seeks God beyond this favour?
symptomes: Joy before the promise.
Feeling that even the reward does not fill the whole desire.
Desiring the Face more than the gifts.
cases.ouverture: Paradise itself can become a call to seek God beyond the gift.

cases.resume: The extinction to limited existence, still incomplete.
cases.explication_simple: He is extinguished to limited (possible) existence, but his thirst is not quenched: he finds no appeasement and senses another extinction.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Extinction within existence makes the attachment to possible existence disappear, but it does not yet fill the thirst. The traveller may believe he has touched the goal when he has only a partial extinction. This prepares the danger of seeking the master in imagination.
cases.mecanisme: The desire for the lifting of the veil pushes towards a form of extinction, but without a guide it remains ambiguous.
cases.illusion_possible: Confusing a partial extinction with the ultimate realisation.
remede: Not conclude too quickly.
Ask for a guide and a verification.
Return to the humility of the stage.
cases.question_introspection: Which experience have I taken for an end when it still calls for guidance?
cases.pratique: After a strong inner experience, avoid making it an identity; present it to God as a stage to be purified.
symptomes: Detachment from ordinary forms.
An unquenched thirst despite a strong experience.
A tendency to name as goal what is only a stage.
cases.ouverture: This extinction teaches that high states still need discernment.

cases.resume: Imaginal identification with the absent master.
cases.explication_simple: Deprived of the living master, he imagines and evokes him until he “extinguishes” himself in him — “the shaykh told me… I am Shaykh So-and-so.” But this is only the displacement of one person into another, which does not draw nearer to the goal.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Extinction within the master can become the displacement of one person into another. The traveller imagines the shaykh, speaks as if conversing with him, even identifies himself with him. Without real presence and without adab, this extinction does not draw nearer to the goal.
cases.mecanisme: The thirst for extinction, deprived of an accessible guide, seeks the master in the imagination.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that identifying with the guide replaces real obedience and verification.
remede: Distinguish beneficial evocation from imaginary appropriation.
Return to the Law, to adab and to deeds.
Seek real guidance rather than a projection.
cases.question_introspection: What image of a guide am I using to avoid a real obedience?
cases.pratique: When an inner spiritual figure speaks, check whether it produces humility, obedience and sobriety, or inflation.
symptomes: Fabricating inwardly a dialogue with the master.
Authorising oneself through an imagined presence.
Confusing psychic identification with spiritual transformation.
cases.ouverture: Seeing this danger makes one more sober in one's relation to the master and more attached to the real.

cases.resume: Outward devotion that can deny the inner reality.
cases.explication_simple: Belief in the outward teachings of the Law alone and their practice: the literalist devotee who denies all inner meaning and holds what the Sufis say to be imagination.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The kingdom of devotion affirms the outward teachings of the Law, but can close itself to the realities of Sufism by treating them as imagination. It can be seized by pride or self-satisfaction and fall back towards false devotion.
cases.mecanisme: The failure of imaginal extinction can bring one back to a defensive outward devotion.
cases.illusion_possible: To take the refusal of subtleties for perfect fidelity to the Law.
remede: Keep the Law without denying the heart.
Avoid the pride of outward practice.
Ask for a balanced understanding of the zāhir and the bāṭin.
cases.question_introspection: Does my outward fidelity open my heart or close it?
cases.pratique: Identify a domain where I judge what I do not understand, and entrust it to God with prudence rather than with contempt.
symptomes: Reducing religion to what one masters.
Despising inner realities out of fear of illusion.
Believing oneself solid because one refuses all depth.
cases.ouverture: This station recalls that the Law and the inner reality must protect one another.
cases.chemin_texte: He is then seized by the grapnel of pride (al-kibr) or, according to a variant, of self-satisfaction (al-ʻujb), which drags him down and makes him descend twenty-six degrees, passing through the ruins (No. 45), evil deeds (No. 9), hatred (No. 8), to fall into false devotion (No. 51).

cases.resume: The largesse of goods and of prestige.
cases.explication_simple: He is generous with his goods and his prestige: generosity is a divine characteristic — “the generous one is near to God, to paradise” — which leads to the paradise of knowledge.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Generosity has a usefulness without limit and shares in a noble quality. It leads to the paradise of knowledge when it is assumed as a character received from God. It frees one from the constriction of possession.
cases.mecanisme: After the tensions of devotion, the heart can open through giving; what was held back circulates and becomes a passage of knowledge.
cases.illusion_possible: To give in order to conquer, dominate, or be praised as generous.
remede: Give without buying hearts.
Give also of one's prestige when it can protect.
Recall that every gift received comes from God.
cases.question_introspection: What do I hold back because I seek my security in it?
cases.pratique: Make a real gift, material or symbolic, that costs enough to touch the attachment yet remains just.
symptomes: Giving goods, time or influence.
Rejoicing in letting what is received circulate.
Identifying less with possession.
cases.ouverture: Generosity opens onto a wider knowledge because it shatters the idol of possession.

cases.resume: The Reality recognised after having been denied.
cases.explication_simple: The Reality (ḥaqīqa) which he previously denied: he perceives the existence of an undeniable reality, as undeniable as the presence of the spirit in the body. (Variant: knowledge.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: The Reality is what the traveller denied when he reduced the path to the outward alone. It becomes evident like the presence of the spirit in the body: real, even though its deep nature escapes him. This square opens the door to knowledge.
cases.mecanisme: Generosity and the opening of the heart make perceptible a reality that the defensive discourse denied.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that perceiving the Reality dispenses with tradition and form.
remede: Welcome the evidence without pretension.
Relate it to the transmitted science.
Do not oppose experience and tradition.
cases.question_introspection: What reality have I long denied because it disturbed my framework?
cases.pratique: Name an inner reality that has become evident and seek how tradition illumines it.
symptomes: Sensing an undeniable spiritual reality.
No longer being able to reduce the path to the outward.
Seeking to clothe inner knowledge in a transmitted form.
cases.ouverture: The Reality recognised reconciles the spiritual world, reason and transmission.

cases.resume: Knowledge of the Creator by distinction from creation.
cases.explication_simple: He knows that this world has a Creator (mukawwin) who is distinct from all and is qualified by every perfection: “God is existent, no doubt in that.” (Variant: the reality.)
cases.explication_spirituelle: Knowledge seeks the Creator of the world, distinct from all that is not He, qualified by every perfection and free of every imperfection. The traveller understands that he must know the proof which is the manifested world, without confusing proof and proven.
cases.mecanisme: The Reality recognised arouses the desire to know the One who founds this reality.
cases.illusion_possible: To take the world for God, or to seek God outside of every sign.
remede: Contemplate the signs without absolutising them.
Purify the doctrine of God of resemblances.
Ask for a knowledge that increases reverence and obedience.
cases.question_introspection: Do I stop at the traces, or do I let them point to their Source?
cases.pratique: Observe a sign of the world and formulate what it shows, without confusing it with God.
symptomes: Seeking God through the signs of the world.
Distinguishing creation from Creator.
Not being satisfied with a vague belief.
cases.ouverture: Knowledge becomes an ordered quest: to know the proof in order to ascend to the Creator.

cases.resume: The manifested world, coarse and subtle, as present proof.
cases.explication_simple: The manifested world (al-kawn), where God is present: he observes that manifestation divides into coarse (kathīf) and subtle (laṭīf).
cases.explication_spirituelle: The manifested world is the place where the traveller finds himself. He observes that manifestation comprises the coarse and the subtle. This distinction leads him towards the spirit, nearer to the decree.
cases.mecanisme: Knowledge of the Creator requires looking at the world as proof and not as absolute.
cases.illusion_possible: To reduce the manifested world to its material density alone, or to lose oneself in the subtle without discernment.
remede: Observe without idolising.
Distinguish coarse and subtle.
Let manifestation lead towards the spirit.
cases.question_introspection: Which sign of the manifested world am I invited to read more finely?
cases.pratique: In a concrete situation, seek its visible coarse aspect and its invisible subtle aspect.
symptomes: Seeing the degrees of the visible and the subtle.
Understanding that manifestation is not uniform.
Seeking what animates the form.
cases.ouverture: The world becomes a book of signs when it is read in its degrees.

cases.resume: The spirit, subtle creature of the world of the decree.
cases.explication_simple: The spirit (al-rūḥ): one of the most marvellous creatures, the nearest to God and the first created; it belongs to the world of the decree (ʻālam al-amr) — “The Spirit comes from the decree of my Lord” (XVII, 85).
cases.explication_spirituelle: The spirit is presented as a marvellous creature, near to God and pertaining to the decree. It opens towards the Word and the Attribute. This square invites one to respect the mystery of the spirit without claiming to grasp its essence.
cases.mecanisme: The distinction of the coarse and the subtle leads to recognising the spirit as the subtle principle of life.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe one knows the spirit because one speaks of it.
remede: Keep reverence before the mystery of the spirit.
Do not speculate beyond what is given.
Relate the spirit to obedience and not to curiosity.
cases.question_introspection: Is my relation to the spirit reverence, or possessive curiosity?
cases.pratique: Meditate on the breath and on life without seeking to possess their secret, then give thanks for this decree.
symptomes: Sensing a life deeper than the body.
Questioning oneself about the divine decree.
Feeling that the spirit escapes ordinary holds.
cases.ouverture: The spirit orients one towards the divine Word and towards what surpasses matter.

cases.resume: The Presence of the significations of the essence, the names and the qualities.
cases.explication_simple: The divine world (al-lāhūt): the Presence that encompasses the significations of the essence, the names and the qualities.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The divine world, or lāhūt, designates a Presence encompassing the divine significations. It is not an object to be possessed but an orientation of knowledge. The traveller enters a higher vocabulary, which demands still more modesty.
cases.mecanisme: The contemplation of the spirit leads towards the Presence that encompasses essence, names and qualities.
cases.illusion_possible: To speak of the divine world as if one dominated what it designates.
remede: Keep the language sober.
Rely on the masters and the texts.
Let knowledge increase adoration.
cases.question_introspection: Does my language about God increase my modesty or my self-assurance?
cases.pratique: Choose a divine Name and meditate on it as a sign, without claiming to enclose the Presence it indicates.
symptomes: Being drawn by the divine significations.
Sensing the unity behind the names and qualities.
Needing sure references so as not to lose oneself.
cases.ouverture: The lāhūt widens knowledge while recalling the inaccessibility of the mystery.

cases.resume: The Presence of the mystery, the enveloping source of the names and qualities.
cases.explication_simple: The Immensity (al-jabarūt): the Presence of the mystery (al-ghayb), source of all things, self-sufficient, where the names and the qualities are enveloped.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The Immensity, jabarūt, is the Presence of the mystery and of the source. All is enveloped therein without yet being unfolded as creature. This square teaches respect for the principial invisible and the impossibility of reducing it.
cases.mecanisme: The divine Presence of the significations opens towards the more encompassing mystery, there where the names, qualities and creatures are enveloped at their source.
cases.illusion_possible: To take the Immensity for a domain that one can map out mentally.
remede: Fall silent before what surpasses.
Do not transform the mystery into a system.
Return to humble adoration.
cases.question_introspection: Do I know how to let God be greater than my understanding?
cases.pratique: Before a metaphysical question too high, prefer a humble invocation to a hasty conclusion.
symptomes: The sense of being before an immensity that cannot be mastered.
The silence of discursive intelligence.
The perception that the names and qualities have an enveloping source.
cases.ouverture: The Immensity educates the soul to silence, to reverential awe and to surrender.

cases.resume: Extinction to all that is not God.
cases.explication_simple: Extinction (fanāʾ) to all that is not He: he hears by the Hearing of God, sees by the Sight of God, perceives the scratching of the pens and the descent of the decrees.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Extinction in God is described as a station where the servant hears and sees by God, perceives the decrees and the subtle realities. It is an immense summit, yet not yet guaranteed against later dangers. The higher the station, the more adab must be kept.
cases.mecanisme: The Immensity of the mystery consumes attachment to all that is not God; the heart ceases to take things for rival poles.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that extinction once lived makes any future fall impossible.
remede: Keep the secret of one's states.
Return to servanthood and to the Law.
Do not conclude that the path is complete.
cases.question_introspection: Even in my highest states, where can the self still hide?
cases.pratique: Faced with a state of effacement or deep peace, ask to be kept in adab rather than to be confirmed in the exceptional.
symptomes: The effacement of the heart's rival objects.
A finer perception of the decrees.
The risk of speaking too loftily of a station too lofty.
cases.ouverture: True extinction radically decentres the servant from himself.

cases.resume: The reception of protected sciences and wisdoms.
cases.explication_simple: God communicates to him sciences and secrets, teaches him Wisdom and forbids him to divulge it; He protects him against disobedience.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Prophethood is here a station of communication of sciences and secrets, with a prohibition against divulging and protection against disobedience. For a reader, it must be approached with great prudence: it indicates an elevated function, not a model to be claimed.
cases.mecanisme: After extinction, God may communicate sciences and wisdoms according to what He wills.
cases.illusion_possible: To imagine oneself the trustee of a prophetic function or of a secret to be proclaimed.
remede: Never claim a rank.
Protect what must remain unsaid.
Measure science against obedience.
cases.question_introspection: Do I want to receive in order to obey, or to receive in order to be recognised?
cases.pratique: When a strong understanding arrives, ask whether it should be spoken, kept, or transformed into a silent act.
symptomes: Understanding that certain sciences must not be exposed.
Sensing the link between knowledge and protection.
Respecting the spiritual ranks.
cases.ouverture: This station teaches that the higher the science, the more it demands secrecy, protection and obedience.

cases.resume: Sainthood as the protection of God and a response of obedience.
cases.explication_simple: God surrounds him with His protection (wilāya) and he himself responds to Him through obedience.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The wilāya is presented soberly: God surrounds with His protection and the servant responds through obedience. It is not spectacle but a guarded nearness. This square corrects the fantasies about sainthood.
cases.mecanisme: The protected science leads towards a nearness where God keeps His servant and where the servant responds through a more delicate obedience.
cases.illusion_possible: To confuse sainthood with powers, prestige or exception.
remede: Seek obedience more than signs.
Render the protection back to God.
Avoid all staging of nearness.
cases.question_introspection: Do I desire to be near to God, or to be seen as near?
cases.pratique: Ask today not for a sign of sainthood, but for a purer obedience in some simple thing.
symptomes: A more spontaneous obedience.
The sense of being kept rather than powerful.
Discretion regarding the gifts received.
cases.ouverture: True sainthood is recognised by protection and obedience, not by display.

cases.resume: The return towards subtle manifestation: spirits, angels and intimacy.
cases.explication_simple: The return towards subtle manifestation: he sees the spirits and the angels. A feeling of intimacy (uns) with God comes to him after the arduous seeking and the effort.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The Dominion is a descending return towards the subtle manifested. The traveller sees spirits and angels and tastes an intimacy after the effort. It teaches that the ascent must also return towards the created worlds without losing God.
cases.mecanisme: Protected sainthood allows a return towards subtle manifestation without rupture with God.
cases.illusion_possible: To become attached to the subtle worlds as to a new possession.
remede: Do not seek the angels for their own sake.
Remain in gratitude.
Keep the Law as anchor.
cases.question_introspection: Does what is subtle orient me towards God or towards fascination?
cases.pratique: When a subtle peace appears, give thanks and return to a concrete act of obedience.
symptomes: Intimacy after a long struggle.
Attention to subtle presences.
Rest after the spiritual effort.
cases.ouverture: The Dominion shows that the subtle can be traversed in intimacy with God.

cases.resume: The return to coarse manifestation and to the human world.
cases.explication_simple: The return to coarse manifestation: he completes his descending return towards the Kingdom (al-mulk). His return is only bodily; his spirit is like that of the martyrs, living with their Lord.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The nāsūt is the return towards coarse manifestation, the Kingdom. After the heights, the servant returns to the world of man. This descent is not a fall if it is made with the intimacy acquired and with fidelity.
cases.mecanisme: After subtle manifestation, the traveller completes the descending return towards the concrete human.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that returning to the human means losing realisation.
remede: Honour the ordinary duties.
Do not oppose the everyday and spirituality.
Keep presence in simple gestures.
cases.question_introspection: How can my concrete human life carry what I have understood?
cases.pratique: Choose a very ordinary task and accomplish it as a place of return to God.
symptomes: Returning to human responsibilities.
Carrying an inner intimacy into the everyday.
No longer despising the ordinary world.
cases.ouverture: The human world becomes the place where knowledge is verified.

cases.resume: Creation subsisting through the Real, and the Real manifesting through creation.
cases.explication_simple: The station (ḥaḍra) of creation subsisting through the Real — or of the Real manifesting through creation. God makes known to him the world of the secret with no intermediary.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Testimonial vision gives knowledge of the world of the secret and of vision with no intermediary. It is a very high station: it can free one from dependence on creatures, but it can also precede a major danger of self-sufficiency.
cases.mecanisme: The intimacy acquired in the Dominion and the return to the human open onto a vision of the real within creation.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that a vision without intermediary renders one autonomous from all adab.
remede: Keep humility after the visions.
Do not attribute to oneself the knowledge received.
Remain under the keeping of adab.
cases.question_introspection: Does what I see make me more a servant or more self-sufficient?
cases.pratique: Before a visible thing, seek how it bears witness without giving me a feeling of superiority.
symptomes: Seeing things as present signs.
Sensing that creation subsists through God.
Depending less on the approval of creatures.
cases.ouverture: Testimonial vision can unify secret and manifestation in a deep lucidity.

cases.resume: The self-sufficiency born of stations, sciences and acquired perfections.
cases.explication_simple: He grows proud of counting only on himself, of the stations attained, of the sciences and perfections acquired — the grapnel of trial seizes him and casts him down from 82 stations.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Conceit is one of the great warnings of the Chessboard. After real heights, the traveller grows proud of his journey and his secrets. If he settles in there, a grapnel makes him fall very low, down to trial.
cases.mecanisme: Vision without intermediary, if it is appropriated by the self, becomes pride in one's own progress.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that the gifts received are personal properties.
remede: Render the gifts back to God.
Return to the lack of personal power.
Seek a humble correction before the fall.
cases.question_introspection: Of what progress am I secretly proud?
cases.pratique: When a spiritual success returns to memory, accompany it with a request for protection against pride.
symptomes: Believing oneself above the ordinary means.
Recounting one's journey inwardly as proof of superiority.
No longer receiving correction or counsel.
cases.ouverture: Seeing conceit in time saves one from one of the most severe falls.
cases.chemin_texte: If he stops at this station, he is seized by the grapnel of trial (No. 10), which drags him down and makes him descend eighty-two stations.

cases.resume: The station of Isrāfīl, linked to the Guarded Tablet and to the breath of the resurrection.
cases.explication_simple: The presence of the angel to whom is entrusted the Guarded Tablet (al-lawḥ al-maḥfūẓ) and who blows the trumpet: at the first breath all is annihilated save seven realities, at the second all is resurrected.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Isrāfīl introduces one to the sciences of the decree, of annihilation and of resurrection. The traveller approaches the secrets entrusted to the angels, but must learn without attributing their function to himself. The grandeur of the station demands a humility greater still.
cases.mecanisme: If conceit is avoided, the traveller is led towards angelic stations and their sciences.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe oneself master of the secrets of the decree because one contemplates them.
remede: Meditate on the resurrection with fear and hope.
Do not speculate with arrogance upon the decree.
Keep servanthood before the angels.
cases.question_introspection: Does the thought of the resurrection make me more awake, or only more curious?
cases.pratique: Contemplate finitude: what appears stable can be blown away and raised up by God alone.
symptomes: Meditating on the end of creatures and the resurrection.
Sensing the majesty of the preserved decree.
Respecting the angelic functions.
cases.ouverture: Isrāfīl recalls that all created life is suspended upon the command of God.

cases.resume: The station of Gabriel, angel of the Revelation and of the Science.
cases.explication_simple: The presence of the honoured angel to whom are entrusted the Science (ʻilm) and the Revelation (waḥī), the announcement made by God to the messengers and the prophets.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Gabriel is linked to the Science and to the Revelation, the announcement made to the prophets and messengers. The traveller learns a part of the secrets of this function, but must remain in his place as servant. Every revealed science calls for submission.
cases.mecanisme: Knowledge of the angelic stations leads towards the angel of revelation and of science.
cases.illusion_possible: To confuse personal inspiration with revelation.
remede: Submit every inspiration to the revealed framework.
Study with adab.
Do not give one's thoughts the rank of a divine message.
cases.question_introspection: Do I receive science as a trust or as a power?
cases.pratique: Before a strong idea, ask: is it in accord with the Revelation, and does it produce submission?
symptomes: Increased respect for the Revelation.
The desire to understand the divine messages.
The awareness that science descends, it is not invented.
cases.ouverture: Gabriel recalls that true science is received in obedience.

cases.resume: The perfection of Muhammadan servanthood.
cases.explication_simple: The source of Mercy, the mine of the Message, the place of manifestation of Praise: the perfection of servanthood (ʻubūdiyya) fully consented — “Glory to Him who carried His servant by night” (XVII, 1). Servanthood is the noblest and the highest of stations.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The Muhammadan kingdom is the source of mercy, the mine of the Message and the place of Praise. It designates the perfection of consented servanthood, the observance of the rights of Lordship, and adab towards the Lord with consciousness of human weakness. It is a station of return and of major protection.
cases.mecanisme: Useful science or the angelic stations well traversed bring one back towards the perfect servanthood of the prophetic model.
cases.illusion_possible: To seek the spiritual kingdoms while forgetting that the highest rank is servanthood.
remede: Return to servanthood when the heights attract.
Multiply praise and gratitude.
Observe the rights of Lordship in detail.
cases.question_introspection: Do I seek a station more than the perfection of being a servant?
cases.pratique: Perform a simple act of servanthood, rejoicing in being a servant, not the holder of a state.
symptomes: Praise and gratitude that intensify.
The joy of being a servant rather than the owner of stations.
A finer adab towards Lordship.
cases.ouverture: The Muhammadan kingdom unites the highest nearness and the humblest obedience.

cases.resume: The Throne as the epiphanic place of the Magnificence.
cases.explication_simple: The Throne (al-ʻarsh): the epiphanic place of the Magnificence, seat of the theophanic radiance, the supreme aspect and pure Existence (al-wujūd al-muṭlaq). “There is nothing above the Throne but the Merciful”: he sees the Merciful upon the Throne.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The Throne is presented as the place of supreme manifestation, encompassing the categories of beings, with nothing above it but the Merciful. The traveller stands before a majesty that surpasses all appropriation.
cases.mecanisme: Muhammadan servanthood opens onto the contemplation of the Magnificence of the Throne.
cases.illusion_possible: To transform the contemplation of the Throne into speculation or into spiritual prestige.
remede: Keep reverence.
Do not seek to possess the mystery.
Return to the praise of the Merciful.
cases.question_introspection: What does the majesty of the Throne restore to its just measure in me?
cases.pratique: Before what surpasses all measure, practise a simple praise rather than an explanation.
symptomes: A sense of enveloping majesty.
Silence before what dominates the worlds.
The awareness that every created reality is beneath the Merciful.
cases.ouverture: The Throne teaches the immensity of the divine governance and the blessed smallness of the servant.

cases.resume: Permanence in God, distinguished from mere knowledge about it.
cases.explication_simple: The permanence (baqāʾ) that came after extinction (85) was only in himself; it now becomes permanence in God. But the guide admonishes him: if it has come only through knowledge, not through tasting (dhawq), it risks being a gradual fall in disguise (istidrāj).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Permanence in God is immense, but the text places here a very strong warning: it may as yet be known only through knowledge, not through tasting. The guide reminds one to return to the Muhammadan kingdom and to fear istidrāj, the gradual fall disguised as elevation.
cases.mecanisme: After extinction and the contemplation of the Throne, the traveller believes he attains a truer permanence.
cases.illusion_possible: To take a permanence understood for a permanence realised.
remede: Obey the reminder of the guide.
Return to Muhammadan servanthood.
Fear the admiration of one's own opinion.
cases.question_introspection: What realisation proclaimed in me is perhaps still only knowledge?
cases.pratique: When an understanding gives a feeling of stability, ask: is it lived, tasted, corrected, or only known?
symptomes: A feeling of stability in God.
The temptation to believe oneself more advanced than the guide.
Possible confusion between theoretical knowledge and tasted realisation.
cases.ouverture: This station teaches that even the highest must remain correctible and bound to the Muhammadan kingdom.

cases.resume: The kingdom of Abraham: divine friendship, surrender, submission and generosity.
cases.explication_simple: The kingdom of Abraham, the Friend (al-Khalīl): the Merciful takes him as friend; the grace of the Bestower (al-Wahhāb) and of the Benefactor (al-Mannān) is manifested, and submission, surrender (tafwīḍ) and generosity predominate in him.
cases.explication_spirituelle: The kingdom of Abraham manifests the grace of the Bestower and the predominance of submission, of tafwīḍ and of generosity. But on the guided journey, this station must be approached with authorisation and adab, without claiming to possess it.
cases.mecanisme: Permanence in God, if it is not brought back to adab, opens towards stations very high and dangerous through appropriation.
cases.illusion_possible: To attribute to oneself the divine friendship as a personal status.
remede: Do not claim the station.
Return to the Muhammadan kingdom.
Practise generosity and submission without a title.
cases.question_introspection: Do I want divine intimacy, or the title of intimate?
cases.pratique: Perform an act of trusting surrender and of generosity without recounting oneself as a friend of God.
symptomes: The desire for intimacy with God as a friend.
The taste of surrender and generosity.
The risk of believing oneself the owner of an Abrahamic rank.
cases.ouverture: The kingdom of Abraham teaches generous surrender under the keeping of adab.

cases.resume: Mīkāʾīl: the gifts, the rains, the provisions, without independent influence.
cases.explication_simple: The archangel Mīkāʾīl, to whom God has entrusted the rains and the provisions and the management of the divine Treasures: a station where one commands nothing, out of pure regard for God and for His Angel.
cases.explication_spirituelle: Mīkāʾīl is linked to the benefits, the provisions, the oceans and the formation of beings, but he has no independent influence. The danger is to believe one commands the gifts or the angel. This station teaches respect for the angelic mediations without attribution of power.
cases.mecanisme: After Abrahamic friendship, the traveller may approach the functions of the distribution of benefits.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that the one who knows an angelic function commands it.
remede: Give no inner command to the mediations.
Thank God for the benefits.
Remain generous without believing oneself the source.
cases.question_introspection: In which gift do I take myself for the source or the sovereign distributor?
cases.pratique: Before a benefit received or given, clearly name the Source and refuse to make oneself its owner.
symptomes: Being attentive to the gifts poured upon the creatures.
Seeing the divine generosity in the provisions.
The risk of imagining one influences the mediations.
cases.ouverture: Mīkāʾīl recalls that the gifts pass through servants but come from God alone.

cases.resume: ʻIzrāʾīl: the seizing of souls entrusted to an angel, but accomplished by God.
cases.explication_simple: The Angel ʻIzrāʾīl, to whom God has entrusted the seizing of souls: one claims nothing of it, one respects its adab.
cases.explication_spirituelle: ʻIzrāʾīl recalls death and the seizing of souls. The text insists: the angel has no independent influence, God seizes the souls and entrusts this function. The danger is to imagine a power over life and death.
cases.mecanisme: After the benefits and the provisions, the traveller approaches the angelic function linked to the end of created life.
cases.illusion_possible: To believe that knowing death gives an influence over it.
remede: Prepare for death through uprightness, not through fascination.
Refuse all pretension to power.
Pray for a good end.
cases.question_introspection: Does the thought of death make me more humble, or more fascinated by power?
cases.pratique: Make a brief meditation on death that increases tenderness, the urgency of good, and the entrusting of oneself to God.
symptomes: Meditating on death as an entrusted decree.
Sensing the fragility of every living soul.
The risk of fascination with the powers of life and death.
cases.ouverture: This station makes one humble before the sovereignty of God over souls.

cases.resume: The ultimate temptation: to believe oneself influential over things and to depart from servanthood.
cases.explication_simple: The station of Satan: the guide leads one there to teach flight towards God. The soul is more dangerous than seventy demons; none vanquishes it save by fleeing towards God (“Flee then towards God!”, LI, 50).
cases.explication_spirituelle: Shayṭān is placed at the end as a major warning: even after the heights, pride and self-sufficiency can make the traveller believe he has an influence over beings. The remedy is the reminder of the condition of servant, flight towards God, the Law of the Prophet, and the return to the Muhammadan kingdom.
cases.mecanisme: The highest stations, if they are appropriated by the self, can culminate in the pretension of influence and independence.
cases.illusion_possible: The greatest illusion is to believe that elevation abolishes servanthood.
remede: Return immediately to the condition of servant.
Cleave to God and to the prophetic Law.
Do not despair: repentance, resumption of the itinerary, request for science, and deeds that efface.
cases.question_introspection: What suggestion makes me believe that I am no longer simply a servant?
cases.pratique: When a thought of exception or of power arises, cut it off by a flight towards God: invocation, return to an obligation, request for protection.
symptomes: Believing oneself to have departed from servanthood.
Imagining one commands beings, gifts or states.
Listening to the suggestions that flatter personal exception.
cases.ouverture: Even this station can become an awakening if it reveals that the soul is more dangerous than the demons and that one must flee towards God.
